{{Quote|I'm no Jedi; I'm just a guy with a lightsaber and a few questions.|Kyle Katarn|audio=GuyWithALightsaber.flac|<ref name="jk2" />}}
'''Kyle Katarn''' was a famous [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operative from [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] and later a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] of the [[New Jedi Order]]. He was a former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] who defected to the Rebellion. Often paired with fellow Rebel agent and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jan Ors]], he performed many covert missions for the Rebel Alliance and later the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], including the sabotage of the [[Dark Trooper Project]].

Katarn was later informed of his [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitivity]], and taught himself to become a Jedi while in search of the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. During this journey, he confronted the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] within both himself and in the form of the [[High Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and his minions. After defeating them, Katarn went on to become a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Despite this, after a mission to investigate an ancient temple in [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] Katarn was deluded by its influence and temporarily fell to the dark side; however, thanks to his padawan, Mara Jade, he was redeemed and was able to return to the light.

Mentally scarred from this experience, Katarn chose to cut himself from [[the Force]], forsaking his role in the New Jedi Order and going back to his life as a mercenary. It wasn't until Jan was seemingly killed by the rogue [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Desann]] that Katarn decided to use the force once again and recover his discarded [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], thwarting the [[Empire Reborn campaign|Empire Reborn's campaign]], forming a friendship with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], rescuing Ors and stopping both the new [[Shadowtrooper|Shadow Trooper]] Program and the [[Chistori]]'s plan to destroy the entire Academy once and for all. Katarn was able to return to his position as a Jedi Master and continued teaching at the [[Jedi Praxeum]].

He participated in the offensives against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] and continued to be a vital ally to The New Republic helping against the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Caedus]] during the Second Galactic Civil War, and beyond, becoming a part of the [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Jedi High Council]] in the [[New Jedi Order]] as well for years to come.

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|I want you to remember, son, when you're at the Academy, how very proud I am of you.|Morgan's words to Kyle the day they parted|<ref name="agent" />}}
[[File:YoungKatarn.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A recording of Katarn with his father before leaving for the Imperial Academy.]]
Kyle Katarn was the son of [[Morgan Katarn|Morgan]] and [[Patricia Katarn]], [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] from the [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] colony moon of [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]].<ref name="sfte" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] in [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]],<ref name="Kyle" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|boy]] grew up on the [[Farm/Legends|farm]]. Ηis mother's death, caused by a malfunctioning [[BT-16 perimeter droid|BT-16]] perimeter security droid, was devastating for the Katarns, and the two men, alone, had to struggle without her extra hands.<ref name="Saga1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> He found a brother of sorts in his father's custom-made labor [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[WeeGee]] with whom they could communicate in [[binary/Legends|droidspeak]]. He spent a lot of time with his father in their workshop where they repaired or assembled machines, an occupation that bonded them even closer. He was fascinated with his father's tools and what he could make with them; since he was twelve, he maintained WeeGee himself, and soon after he could disassemble and repair anything in the homestead.<ref name="agent">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref> His father also taught him a [[Primitive binary code]] which he used to send signals to their [[agrirobot/Legends|agrirobots]].<ref name="JKaudio" />

As a kid, Kyle used to play [[Hide-and-seek/Legends|hide-and-seek]] in a canyon and cavern; shortly before turning eighteen, the locals had begun surveying that area in order to install a [[Geothermal tap|G-Tap]].<ref name="sfte" /> Eventually, Katarn made his father a present of a [[Multi-tool/Legends|multitool]] with his initials engraved on the handle.<ref name="agent" />

Once he came of age, Kyle wished to alleviate some financial burden from his father,<ref name="Saga1" /> but also gain enough knowledge so that he would be able to continue the family trade as an agro-tech mechanic in the future.<ref name="DFmanual">''[[Dark Forces Manual: Coded Transmissions]]''</ref> As a [[Rimmer/Legends|Rimmer]], Kyle had few opportunities to obtain an education and the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] was one of them; his father (whom Kyle knew was a bit too sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]), reluctantly agreed. In [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Characters">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>, he enrolled at the Academy and left his homeworld on a [[Carida/Legends|Carida]]-bound [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]].<ref name="sfte" /> The day before his departure, his father gifted him an antique [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]];<ref name="Saga1" /> Kyle kept with him a holo-recording of their last meeting, where Morgan expressed how proud he was for him.<ref name="agent" />

During his four-year training as an [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] student in the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]], he discovered his natural gift for [[cybernetics/Legends|cybernetics]] systems theory and information engineering.<ref name="DFmanual" /><ref name="Saga1" /> As all engineers, Kyle was trained in only thirty hours in [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], concentrating more to piloting several different kinds of support craft such as [[T-4 (vehicle)|T-4]]s.<ref name="agent" /><ref name="sfte" /> Other activities included extra-curricular martial arts<ref name="DFmanual" /> such as [[fencing]] from a moderate instructor who however required obedience,<ref name="agent" /> target-shooting classes<ref name="DFmanual" /> studying battle tactics with set-pieces<ref name="dfjk" /> and tiring patrols inside an unbearable [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|Stormtrooper armor]], a burden that took a toll on their morale and readiness.<ref name="agent" /> His roommate was [[Meck Odom|Odom Meck]], and he had to cope with the annoying nobleman [[Nathan Donar III]].<ref name="sfte" /> He often crossed paths with [[Jan Ors|Jan Strange]] of the [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intell]], who was actually a Rebel mole.<ref name="Saga2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}}</ref><ref name="DFmanual" />

Three months before his graduation, Kyle, like many other cadets, had focused on the lethal tradition of "walking the edge" during their ceremonial parade, and made research; he found out that thirty-six cadets had found their deaths falling from the cliffside, almost half of them marching on the right flank, in which he would eventually be assigned. Nathan Donar was assigned Company Commander who would lead his column.<ref name="sfte" />

His [[Omega Exercise]] was a turning point for his life and career; it consisted of a [[Battle of AX-456|raid]] on a Rebel [[AX-456|asteroid base]], which he led as [[Cadet Leader]]. It was there when Katarn began to suspect his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force potential]], a trait passed to him by his father, when he experienced several [[Force vision/Legends|premonitions]] that saved the lives of many of his strike team members. While on the mission, Katarn, showed a concern about the unnecessary [[death/Legends|deaths]] of surrendering Rebel agents, claiming they would be more useful as prisoners; something that annoyed [[Sergeant Major/Legends|Sergeant Major]] [[Hong]]. It was also there when he met again [[Jan Ors]]; Katarn and Ors were on opposite sides of the war, but they experienced a strange connection that led Katarn to spare her life as well as the lives of many of the station's inhabitants. After securing the base, Kyle led his surviving troops to a [[cantina/Legends|cafeteria]] waiting for reinforcements; before their arrival they were caught up by Rebel rescue force where he was injured, one of only seven wounded troopers survived behind a barricade. Eventually the Imperial group arrived and rescued the surviving stormtroopers.<ref name="sfte" />

Following this, Katarn was hospitalized back into the Training Base, where he also achieved a heroic status. He exited the hospital only hours before his graduation, during which he would achieve his commission and hoped to make his father proud; unbeknownst to him, Morgan was a supporter of the Rebel Alliance and had been executed by Inquisitor Jerec.<ref name="sfte" /> Questioning his own performance and strange feelings during his last mission, he hid in the Academy's library and met Odom, who had tackled with religion, and entrusted to him his concerns. His connection to Morgan, and his own Force sensitivity, attracted the interest of Jerec, who visited the Academy for the ceremony.<ref name="SEaudio">[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire'' audio drama]]</ref>

====Graduation====
{{Quote|And, in recognition for his valor, and bravery in the face of the enemy, the Emperor hereby presents Second Lieutenant Kyle Katarn with the Medal of Valor, as well as the Empire's heartfelt gratitude.|[[Rom Mohc]]|<ref name="sfte" />}}
[[File:KyleKatarn-academygrad.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Katarn after graduating from the Imperial Academy.]]
During the graduation ceremony, Katarn paraded at the right flank under the orders of Company Commander Nathan Donar, and despite his worries, Donar drove the parade correctly without any casualties falling over the edge.<ref name="sfte" />

He was decorated with the [[Medal of Valor/Legends|Imperial Medal of Valor]] by General [[Rom Mohc]], and congratulated by a figure dressed in black, whom Kyle supposed to be a government official; he introduced himself as [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]], and expressed a puzzling invitation to seek him whenever Kyle was ready to join [[Dark Jedi/Legends|those with power]]. Katarn felt a chilling cold being near him, and as the medal touched his uniform, he had another strange experience: a power surging through his nerves, giving him views of the area, and others' minds, in great detail; his nerves discharged when the man stepped back. He didn't know that Jerec was the very man who had killed his father, not long earlier.<ref name="sfte" />

After the ceremony, Kyle was approached by his cheering peers who wanted to admire the Medal in close. As, like other rimmers, he had no visitors, he was invited by Meck to join his family for dinner, which he reluctantly accepted, pretending that his motivation was to check out Meck's stunning sister.<ref name="sfte" />

Soon after he received an official [[E-mail|message]] announcing that his father had been killed in a suspected Rebel raid on his homeworld of Sulon. Katarn, recalling the lives he spared on the asteroid base, became extremely hateful towards the Rebellion, believing them to be murderers. Immediately he left for the Office of Cadet Affairs and waited for it to open at dawn.<ref name="sfte" />

Although the Academy refused to extend funeral leave, Katarn accepted a commission in the [[Imperial Special Operations|special operations division]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]].<ref name="DFmanual" /> Now a [[Second Lieutenant/Legends|Second Lieutenant]], he took a cruise on the ''[[Star of Empire]]'', his last bit of freedom before officially becoming an officer in the Imperial navy. He hitched a military transport leaving Carida, and on for the orbital transfer station off [[Dorlon II]]. There he boarded a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] which docked to the liner's hangar.<ref name="sfte" />

===Change of heart===
====A fateful cruise====
It was the first time after some years that Kyle was found among non-human beings and could admire females; resulting in an embarrassing moment when the mother of the [[Twin/Legends|twin]] girls frowned at him, and he tripped. To his surprise he realized that people intentionally bumped him; a businessman he met, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], told him that the "real galaxy" is not like the settled environment of the Academy, and his [[Imperial_officer's_uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]] would cause some sneering.<ref name="SEaudio" /> Right then he was reunited with Nathan, who was also traveling with his family, and invited him to dinner, eager to introduce to his parents the heroic "cadet who won the medal"; Kyle was flattered, considering they belonged to the Empire's inner circle. Governor [[Dol Donar II]] showed genuine curiosity for Katarn's achievements, and in turn, Kyle pretended to compliment Nathan's leadership during the parade. They dined in the [[Nebula Room]], where Kyle had green salad and [[candied insects]]. Asked about his roots he mentioned his father and his death and upon hearing this, Donar started a Rebel-hating rant, ensuring Katarn that his father will be avenged as the Emperor has [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|something]] in store for the Rebels. Calrissian then approached them and invited the Governor for a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]].<ref name="sfte" />

After the dinner he went to the [[Corellia Room]] to reunite with the Donars playing with Calrissian; soon after he ran into a familiar face. Their eyes met and recognized it was the Rebel girl from the asteroid. She turned away and Kyle tried to follow her among the crowd; not only to arrest her, but also to seek revenge, and also some answers. Catching on a moving walkway, he started jogging, and into a lift tube. He realized that the level below was off limits to passengers and he used the emergency ladderway; once he landed, he was immobilized by [[Rosco Ross]], an accomplice of the girl who was waiting for him and was led into a storeroom. After the brief brawl, Ors pointed out that he had spared their lives earlier; in turn Katarn also felt another connection with Ors, not the hatred he expected. After exchanging names, Ors agreed to let him live, if he promised that they would part ways and not do anything against them. Kyle felt a desperation and along his promise he asked to talk to her.<ref name="sfte" />

The two met again in the ship's library while she was practicing her art of dancing in a [[virtual reality]] set, causing him to genuinely applaud her talent. They continued to the ship's cafeteria where Katarn wanted to bring up the matter of war and that the people the Rebels were fighting were good men, including the Stormtroopers, and his innocent father. Ors's attention was grabbed when he dropped the name Sulon and she realized that the Imperial was the son of one of their leaders.<ref name="sfte" />

====Defection====
{{Quote|…it is precisely his experience in Imperial service that has turned him solidly against the Empire, and will, I believe, eventually convince him of the necessity of the Alliance.|Jan Ors, to Mon Mothma|<ref name="Dark Forces Manual: Coded Transmissions">''[[Dark Forces Manual: Coded Transmissions]]''</ref>}}
Upon her invitation, Katarn came discreetly to her cabin so that informants won't spot him with a Rebel agent. There he was startled as her droid, [[A-Cee]] began a self-destruct sequence, which was overridden in time by Ors; it was programmed to detonate in the presence of an Imperial Officer. Ors explained her role in the Alliance Intelligence and showed Katarn footage from the [[Battle of the G-Tap]] that had been obtained by the droid and the reporter [[Candice Ondi]] it was serving. Katarn started watching the holorecord in skepticism until he saw his father, leading other Sulonese known to him. Then he realized that it was in fact the Imperials who had staged the attack; Rebel insignias were painted on their ships to make it seem as if the Rebels had been the aggressors. Their use of standard Imperial tactics, which Katarn had studied extensively at the Academy, gave them away. Seeing the footage for himself, Katarn became distrustful towards the Empire and defected, symbolically tearing off his decoration representing the Medal of Valor, throwing it in a recycle bin, thanking her for showing him the truth.<ref name="sfte" />

Sometime later, Kyle sought Ors and found her at the ship's hangar, struggling with a heavy metallic casing, and he helped her lower it to the floor. He said that she should had used a [[floor jack]] or called for a [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droid]], which was exactly what she was thinking, maddening her. He complimented her for her set of tools and offered to help repairing [[Truly Sorry|her ship]], owing to his technical training at the Academy. He some diagnostics on the [[Hyperdrive motivator/Legends|hyperspace motivator]]; an hour passed, and Jan admired his knowledge and surety. She was further impressed by the fact that Kyle finished the task so quickly; Katarn explained that whenever he was letting his mind go, the solution was always becoming clear to him.<ref name="SEaudio" /> Finally Kyle confessed his desire to join the Alliance, his commitment obvious in his eyes. Ors's team was bound to leave the ''Star'' two days later, and she invited him to come with them.<ref name="sfte" />

The next morning, he received an anonymous [[e-mail]], advising him to not waste his career for a Rebel woman, and to be prepared for interrogation; he immediately realized it was sent from Nathan, and his father somehow learned what was going on. Kyle didn't know that their movements were observed by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Slyder]], who sold them to the Donars. Feeling guilty for betraying Nathan's confidence, but not forgetting the reason that made him defect, he called Jan and warned her that someone is after them. He wore his Imperial sidearm and loaded his possessions in a carryall and ran to the hangar, leaving his officer uniforms behind.<ref name="sfte" />

Rosco and Waller were already at the ship, waiting for Ors. As Kyle claimed to be able to fly it, and remembered the access codes from Jan, he briefly took lead and offered to initialize the ship until Ors arrived, surprising both Rebels. His first problem was that the [[Pressure doors/Legends|pressure doors]] were closed, and Controller didn't cooperate without a previous request. Kyle nonetheless activated the [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsors]] and had Waller shoot at another ship to force the Controller to open the doors. As the ''Sorry'' was lurching up and down, he saw Jan entering, followed by a [[Slyder|Rodian they met earlier]], Nathan, and Imperials; he was happy that Nathan wasn't harmed when they exchanged fire. Jan collapsed because of the loss of [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] and he moved the ship towards her, able to focus thanks to an [[Breath mask/Legends|oxygen mask]]. Once Rosco brought Jan on the ramp, and Kyle lifted them up. Men in [[Environment suit/Legends|space suits]] boarded a ship but he didn't fire; they threw a [[Concussion grenade/Legends|concussion grenade]] against the ''Sorry''. Ignoring the controller's threats, he thrusted the ship towards the half-opened doors and exited to space. Ors sat next to him and Waller notified them that a [[Dol Donar's yacht|ship]] was after them; Kyle regretted for not thinking to update the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navcomp]]. The enemy ship was gaining, and Kyle was surprised to see Jan making a turn to confront it; when unexpectedly it blew up by itself. They both realized that it was an internal explosion, caused by A-Cee, having being loaded in the ship, self-destructed in the presence of Nathan.<ref name="sfte" />

====Defection====
{{Quote|Katarn was once an impressive Imperial officer, but he was weak and gave up on the struggle for our New Order.|General [[Rom Mohc]]|<ref name="df" />}}
[[File:Katarn_Carlisle.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Katarn converted into a mercenary serving the Rebel Alliance.]]
The ''Sorry'' made thus a clean escape and they reached the ''[[New Hope]]''. Kyle was impressed by the size of the stolen and refit ship, and how casually the Rebels behaved. Ors introduced him to [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], the leader of the Alliance, where he learned that his father was an esteemed leader, was a friend (and appreciated by) [[Qu Rahn/Legends|of a Jedi]], and explained to Mothma that his reason to become an agent was to avenge him. Mothma showed understanding pointing out that the true and more immediate evil is [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]; to find his father's murderers he should first aid the Rebellion. This invitation brought him skepticism, fear and pride. Mothma revealed to him the existence of the Empire's [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], a space station with a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] capable of destroying an entire planet. Kyle both admired the level of [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]], and also understood Donar's words mentioning the Emperor's plans against the Rebellion. Mothma proposed a mission to him; infiltrate an Imperial facility in the city of [[Trid]] on [[Danuta]] and retrieve [[Death Star plans/Legends|schematics]] of that lethal station, in the hope that Rebel engineers will identify a weak spot. Mothma thought that his Imperial training, and a man in an all-male facility were assets for what could be a suicide mission. Kyle wondered why a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] raid was not an option, and Mothma explained that they want to avoid collateral damage to Trid, a consideration that separates the Empire from the Alliance.<ref name="sfte" />

She also provided him with a video of his main target: the room where the memory matrix with the plans is kept; in the video he spotted a familiar officer, and after replaying it, he realized he was Meck. Mothma inquired whether Kyle knew him, and he lied that he didn't, with sweat visible on his forehead; Mothma however knew the officer's identity and about their friendship but showed no sign of it. Kyle accepted the mission, only for the sake of his father, and the Sulonese. This started his career as a Rebel-sympathetic [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] and [[Spy/Legends|spy]].<ref name="sfte" />

Soon after his recruitment into the Alliance, it is said that Kyle spent some time in a Rebel base populated by smugglers and pirates and acquired skills of sabotage and subterfuge.<ref name="DFmanual" /> Katarn was responsible for introducing the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Rianna Saren]] to the organization. He first tasked her with infiltrating a [[Black Sun warehouse]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to discover how they were involved with the Empire. After Saren successfully completed this mission, Katarn revealed to her that he was a Rebel agent and introduced her to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]].<ref name="lethalliance">''[[Star Wars: Lethal Alliance]]''</ref>

Before departing, he received a laconic e-mail from Jan wishing him good luck. Delivering his orders to the [[Deck Master (New Hope)|Deck Master]], he was assigned some old ships to choose from, and after a lengthy inspection and comparison of their drives, systems, durability and weapons, he chose the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] titled ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]''. He then spent several days equipping the ship with several parts, tools and facilities (some of which he secretly "borrowed"). Furthermore, he was prepared to pose as the pilot '''Dan Drexel''', with forged documentation, before taking off to Danuta.<ref name="sfte" />

====Mission to Danuta====
The ''Moldy Crow'' took five days to take him to his destination, and Kyle, with the decks' vibrations and sounds from the vents as his only company, passed his time doing maintenance works and sleeping; he tried to make plans and scenarios, but because of the lack of more information, he decided that he would have to improvise. As he entered the planet's orbit, he was flanked by two [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIEs]] and identified himself to the [[Orbital patrol vessel]] [[X-Ray 291]]. He was given clearance and was sent approach vectors to the [[Trid spaceport]], which was his first ever planetfall. Before disembarking, he preset the emergency start-up sequence, and was prepared to bribe the local Imperial customs agent.<ref name="sfte" /> The agent pointed out that Kyle's face seemed familiar, seeing it from some debriefing earlier; Kyle claimed that, as he was pulled off hyperspace by an uncharted [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity well]], and was off-schedule, he needed to pass the procedure quickly and gave some [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his trouble.<ref name="SEaudio" />

He spent his first day on [[Trid]] in recon work, passing by a square and walking towards the Facility. He briefly considered abducting a scientist and used them to gain access but he discarded it. He had a strange premonition that he was followed; and entered a [[Cafe/Legends|cafe]] to rest and scan the environment, and indeed saw a familiar figure distantly; he didn't recognize that it was Jan stalking him. Eventually he reached the security perimeter near the Research Complex and pretended to be exercising, but as he wasn't dressed for it, he took a quiet street and retraced his way back to the spaceport, buying food from a street vendor. He confirmed that it would be impossible to enter without help from inside and he remembered Meck, and his choice between friendship and duty. After some dinner in the ''Crow'', he slept and had a [[Dream/Legends|nightmare]]: he was a Rebel on AX-456 shooting Stormtroopers who poured from the hatch, and all bodies had Meck's face under the helmet.<ref name="sfte" />

He went to town early in the morning; he knew that Meck was spiritual and sought for a religious place; getting directions from a street vendor, he found and entered a [[Danuta Temple]], but not before looking around. Again, Ors was behind him but he failed to see her. He arranged for a secret meeting with Meck and rested in the ''Crow''. Kyle spent a nervous afternoon and evening in the ''[[Blue Moon]]'', buying drinks, avoiding stares and offers of female companionship; holding his blaster ready in his lap, he hoped that Meck would show up and would not betray him. About an hour later he was ready to give up when Meck arrived. Meck warned him that Kyle has been quite "infamous", and provided him with a wanted poster, with the holo from the Academy's yearbook, and several accusations for crimes. Kyle admitted that he defected, and he explained everything about his mission. Kyle promised to keep Meck's identity secret and that they'd work together for a believable story.<ref name="sfte" />

Meck gave Katarn information that assisted him in completing his mission. He also deactivated the perimeter, allowing Katarn entry into the base. Inside, he got communication from Jan that the ''Crow'' was en route. [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Horst (Keycard)|Horst]] had his men trap Kyle in the south corridor; while covering from fire, Katarn decided to use the same method as when dealing with technical repairs, empty his mind and let himself go. Then he was able to guess his adversaries' positions and where the bolts were being fired, shooting them all accurately while avoiding all fire; the feeling was the same as he had while meeting Jerec in his graduation. Right then he received another message form Jan, saying that the ''Crow'' was en route, and telling him that he was not insane, but the Force was with him. He made his way to the officer holding the keycard, who was sending a [[Walker/Legends|walker]] to anticipate the incoming ''Crow''. Katarn surprised Horst, who wondered how he survived through his men; instead of handing him the keycard, Horst attempted to shoot him, but Kyle shot first. He called Jan to update her and warn that armed reinforcements were coming her way; he suggested to break off but Jan ignored him. Right then communication was cut; not knowing what happened to Jan, Kyle decided that his only option was to continue the mission.<ref name="SEaudio" />

To this end, Kyle [[Mission to Danuta|entered the]] [[Danuta research facility|secret base]] fighting his way through the imperial personnel to recover the piece of the Death Star plans they were guarding. He reached the secure room hoping that Meck would keep his promise and open the door for him; he did and expressed his amazement for reaching this far. He took him into the vault where the plans were kept, and Kyle for a minute felt evil emanating from it. He thanked Meck, saying that he was a hero, and the lives they will save will wash out the death of his comrades. Meck demanded that he doesn't want to see him ever again and reminded Kyle to give him a convincing bruise. Kyle thanked him and left.<ref name="SEaudio" />

The partial plans stolen by Katarn were eventually combined with those captured at [[Toprawa/Legends|Toprawa]], on the Death Star itself, and by Rianna Saren and [[Z-58-0|Zeeo]] after their battle against Imperial [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] master [[Zarien Kheev]] on [[Danuta]],<ref name="lethalliance" /> to create a complete outline of the station.<ref name="df" />

====Fighting the dark troopers====
{{Quote|A new stormtrooper that can take out a Rebel base that quickly? I shoulda' kept working for the Empire.|Kyle Katarn, to Mon Mothma|audio=KeptWorkinForTheEmpire-DF.flac|<ref name="df" />}}
[[File:BeardlessKyleKatarn-PreTalay-SWDF.png|thumb|right|250px|Kyle Katarn during the briefing regarding his [[mission to Talay]].]]
Soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Mon Mothma asked Katarn to investigate the destruction of [[Tak Base]] resulting from the [[Attack on Tak Base]]. Katarn accepted, and, upon request, took on Jan Ors as his mission officer. Katarn's [[Mission to Talay|investigation of the ruins]] revealed the Imperial [[Dark Trooper Project|Dark Trooper project]], led by [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] veteran General [[Rom Mohc]]. Several clues [[Mission to Anoat|led Katarn to]] [[Anoat/Legends|Anoat]], where he captured [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rebus (Galactic Empire)|Rebus]], an infamous Imperial weapons engineer who had been hiding in the [[Anoat City|sewers]] out of paranoia. After [[Mission to Fest (Galactic Civil War)|raiding a]] [[Weapons Research Facility|testing facility]] for [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]] on the planet [[Fest/Legends|Fest]], Katarn was led to the [[Gromas system/Legends|Gromas System]], where the mineral was being mined and the [[Phase I Dark Trooper|first stage of the]] [[Dark Trooper/Legends|Dark Trooper]] was manufactured. After [[Mission to Gromas|destroying the]] [[Gromas Mines|facility]], Katarn had to [[Mission to Orinackra|rescue]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] from an [[Imperial Detention Center|Imperial detention center]] on [[Orinackra]] and [[Mission to Cal-Seti|traveled to]] the [[Ramsees Hed]] docking port on [[Cal-Seti/Legends|Cal-Seti]]. There, he was able to track a smuggling ship that was making runs to the frozen planet of [[Anteevy/Legends|Anteevy]], where the [[Phase II Dark Trooper/Legends|second stage of Dark Trooper]] construction was being completed. [[Mission to Anteevy|Katarn destroyed]] the [[Ice Station Beta/Legends|facility]] on Anteevy and was [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (Dark Trooper campaign)|led to]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], where he located a [[Nava card|nav card]] describing the activities of all the smugglers involved in the Dark Trooper project. Upon leaving the planet, though, Katarn and Ors were briefly captured by [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]].<ref name="df" />

[[File:Kylejan.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kyle Katarn and Jan Ors]]
After defeating several [[Kell dragon/Legends|Kell dragons]] barehanded, Katarn was able to regain the card, rescue Ors, and escape Jabba's space cruiser, [[Star Jewel|the Star Jewel]]. [[Mission to Coruscant (Kyle Katarn)|Katarn and Ors infiltrated]] the [[Imperial Security Operations|ISO]] building on Coruscant, where he was able to decode the nav card. He also fought and defeated [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Using the new information from the card, Katarn and Ors [[Mission to Fuel Station Ergo|tracked one of the smuggler ships]] to the [[Fuel Station Ergo/Legends|Refueling Station Ergo]]. Katarn hijacked this ship and used it to [[Mission to the Executor|sneak onto]] the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], which was the only ship with the information regarding the location of the ''[[Arc Hammer]]'' which was where [[Phase III Dark Trooper|the third and final stage of the Dark Trooper]] was being manufactured. Once on board, he stowed away on a cargo transport en route to the ''Arc Hammer''. After an intense shootout against a Phase III Dark Trooper clad General Mohc, [[Mission to the Arc Hammer|Katarn proceeded to blow up the ''Arc Hammer'']] and escape, destroying the Dark Trooper Project for good. In gratitude for his bravery, Mon Mothma awarded him the [[Star of Alderaan/Legends|Star of Alderaan]].<ref name="df" />

When Jan took an Imperial identity in an attempt to [[Death/Legends|assassinate]] the Head of the [[School of Torturers]], her cover was blown, and Kyle saved her.<ref name="Saga2" />

Katarn was also a member of a [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|SpecForce]] group assembled by the ex-[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Corwin Shelvay/Legends|Corwin Shelvay]], in which he teamed up with Force-sensitives [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]] and [[Erling Tredway]] to [[Assault on the Death Star|sabotage]] the [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] en route to [[D'rinba IV]].<ref name="Saga1" />

As the Death Star neared D'rinba IV, the Rebels attempted to evacuate, using an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] to disable the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Colossus (Star Destroyer)|''Colossus'']]. The Rebels also used their fleet to [[Assault on the Death Star|assault]] the Death Star as Shelvay's operatives attempted to sabotage the weapon.<ref name="IVBG" >''[[Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game]]''</ref>

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game}}
The operatives were successful in preventing the superlaser's firing. However, the false data in possession of the main Rebel Fleet regarding the operational status of the weapon was never corrected, leading to a near-catastrophe at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="IVBG" />
{{Endgame}}

A few years before [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], a [[Unidentified weapons smuggler|smuggler]] ran weapons into an Imperial controlled [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and managed to run afoul of both the Rebel Alliance and another individual. The Alliance sent Katarn to handle the smuggler's operation, during which Katarn and a [[Unidentified Gran (Rimmer's Rest)|Gran]] [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] crossed paths, and engaged in a [[Lightfight|firefight]] that left the Gran with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] injury in his [[Limb/Legends|leg]]. Katarn disposed of the smuggler's operation soon after.<ref name="RAaudio" />

===New Republic===
Working for the Alliance, Katarn had a long list of accomplishments.<ref name="dfjk" /> However as a freelance mercenary he had the reputation of being in both sides, doing some jobs for the Empire and later the [[Imperial Remnant]].<ref name="RAaudio">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref> Katarn also became infamous as a troublemaker on [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]].<ref name="JK" />

Katarn continued to mourn the loss of his father and seeking the one responsible for his death. [[5 ABY/Legends|One year]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Katarn was contacted by [[8t88/Legends|8t88]], an [[Administrative droid/Legends|administrative droid]] who worked as [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]], and appointed a meeting in [[Rimmer's Rest/Legends|Rimmer's Rest]] on Nar Shaddaa. Worried, Ors tried to discourage him from going but Katarn, seemingly persuaded, ignored her warnings and [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (5 ABY)|went on his own]] to the Smuggler's Moon, eager to know who killed his father.<ref name="agent" />

====Trap on Nar Shaddaa====
{{Quote|This has got to be bad for my health.|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="JK" />}}
[[File:Katarn_meets_8t88.jpg|thumb|200px|Extracting information from 8t88.]]
Once there, Katarn waited for the droid in the cantina while rewatching a holorecording of his last parting from his father before leaving for [[Carida]]. The nostalgia was interrupted by the arrival of the droid; on gunpoint, [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] revealed his father was killed by Jerec, the very person who congratulated him during his graduation years ago, a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] with ambitions of restoring the Empire. Right then the droid admitted that he was actually working for the Dark Jedi, and had lured Katarn there in order to help them decode a [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodisc]] which they had salvaged from his very home back on Sulon. Upon Katarn's refusal to cooperate, 8t88 left the cantina with a Gran and [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Rimmer's Rest)|another bounty hunter]] to dispose of him, not knowing that Katarn had bugged him with a [[tracker droid]]. It turned out that the Gran had past grudges against the mercenary. With his Bryar pistol briefly confiscated, Katarn was able to use [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] to reclaim it, and after a brief skirmish he let himself free, and ran after 8t88, following the signal of his tracker.<ref name="dfjk" /><ref name="agent" />

Dodging and fighting various adversaries, he caught up with the droid as he was about to leave on [[Furious (Lambda-class)|his shuttle]]; from distance, Katarn could only shoot the droid's arm off as he waved the holodisk around in order to taunt Katarn, and it fell down the abyss. It was then when Ors caught up with him in the ''Crow'', having followed his tracks. Katarn refused to be "rescued" and told her to wait for him at the top. Katarn managed to climb down the bowels of the city to the base of the platform tower and reclaimed the holodisk. He fought his way back until was ambushed by bounty hunters, before being rescued again by Ors.<ref name="dfjk" /> In the process was helped by a [[Force ghost/Legends|voice]] that guided him. However, he suffered a blaster wound and a concussion. Ors stabilised his wound but during the three-day trip back to the Rebel fleet, Katarn vomited and lost his consciousness. Ors brought him to the [[Hospital ship|medical frigate]] ''[[Mercy (hospital ship)|Mercy]]''.<ref name="agent" />

====Recovery====
[[File:CrowdockedwithMercy.jpg|200px|thumb|left|The ''Moldy Crow'' docked with ''Mercy'', the medical frigate where Katarn and Ors regrouped.]]
Because of Katarn's status as an agent, he would enjoy high-priority treatment in the special [[Ward 114]] reserved for the Special Operations<ref name="agent" /> but because of his questionable past he was listed as "borderline priority". [[Unnamed medic (Ward 114)|The doctor]] responsible also noticed that he was not on assignment when he was injured. Despite this, and the casualties swarming from [[Shackel]], he decided against sending him to lower decks for less effective treatment but accepted him in the special Ward 114.<ref name="RAaudio" />
[[File:KyleBacta_DFJK.jpg|thumb|200px|Katarn healing in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]].]]
About twelve [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, Ors visited him in [[Bacta tank 23|Tank 23]]; curious about what was so important about the holodisk and knowing that their Rebel peers didn't trust him enough, she "borrowed" it. But not before discovering that Kyle secretly kept a 3-D snapshot of her in his [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|wallet]], and she whispered her love to him.<ref name="agent" /><ref name="RAaudio" />

Indeed, as the doctor said, Katarn was soon removed from the tank and found in a medical bed. While he was unconscious, "everywhere, between wake and sleep... with [[the Force]]", he saw a vision with the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of the Jedi [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], a Jedi Purge survivor that perished recently. He told him about Jerec who murdered his father, and the connection of the two with the [[Valley of the Jedi]], a [[Vergence/Legends|nexus of power]] where the spirits of many Jedi and Sith lay trapped; Force-sensitives could draw great energies of the Force from it and should not be reached by Jerec. The figure revealed that he was watching over Kyle for a long time, and he was already in apprenticeship, but should return to his homestead and use the holodisk in order to assess the ways of the Jedi, and reach the Valley.<ref name="agent" /><ref name="RAaudio" /><ref name="dfjk" /> Kyle didn't show that he was awake, preferring to spy movement around the ward with carefully slit eyes; then he saw Jan entering, but instead of coming to him, she stealthily opened his locker and did something with his clothes. Curious, he stood up to find out what she did; he didn't know that she returned the disk she had taken.<ref name="agent" />

Not long after, Jan visited him as he was preparing his things, telling him that the ''Crow'' would stay for some repairs and would stay behind. Asked if he was going after Jerec, Kyle instead said that he would visit his old home, according to the dream he had seen. He asked Jan to meet him on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] once the ''Crow'' was available.<ref name="dfjk" />

====Homecoming====
[[File:Kylekatarn_jasoncourt.jpg|200px|thumb|Katarn with Qu Rahn's lightsaber.]]
Katarn had to return home to continue his quest, and visited his homeworld for the first time since he had left for the Academy. Following secret paths he knew from his childhood, he reached [[Katarn estate|their estate]] but didn't approach, as he saw a [[Seven Dark Jedi|group of people]] [[Mission to Sulon|emptying]] [[Map to the Valley of the Jedi|some stones]] from his home and loading them in a shuttle.<ref name="dfjk" /><ref name="agent" /><ref name="RAaudio" /> Katarn reactivated their family droid WeeGee and loaded the disk, revealing his father's message and a [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|green-bladed lightsaber]] that belonged to [[Qu Rahn]], setting him on the path to become a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. He embarked on a journey to locate the Valley of the Jedi and protect it from a faction of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] led by [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]], who coveted the valley's energy for his own dark ends. Katarn's father, believed to have been killed in the battle of Sulon, had actually died at the hands of Jerec after refusing to reveal the location of the Valley of the Jedi. However, Morgan had etched a map to the valley into the ceiling tiles in the Katarns' house that was later discovered by Jerec. Katarn arrived after the map had been removed.<ref name="agent" />

He infiltrated the [[Government House (Sulon)|Government House]] in the city of [[Barons Hed]], and located the droid [[8t88/Legends|8t88]], who had created a digitized version of the map. Katarn was intercepted by [[Yun]], one of Jerec's dark pupils. Katarn defeated Yun, but he could not kill a disarmed opponent and allowed him to leave; Katarn was soon attacked and had to escape without either version of the map. He tracked 8t88's shuttle to the [[Fuel City]], a fuel station outside Baron's Hed, and he stowed away aboard Jerec's freighter, the ''[[Sulon Star]]''. While on board, Katarn encountered and defeated two more of Jerec's pupils, the symbiotic "twins" [[Gorc]] and [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], who had recently destroyed 8t88 to dispose of any witnesses to the Valley's location. After defeating Gorc and Pic, Katarn killed [[Grendel]] and took the droid's severed head and managed to escape the vast ship and be picked up by the ''Crow''.<ref name="agent" />

Kyle's troubled escape was followed by a pursuit by TIE fighters from the [[Fuel City]]. Kyle advised Jan to move towards the [[Nefra Canyons]] to lose the TIEs and exited to the ship's hatch. Using the Force, he managed a precise hit with his blaster on the pilot's [[viewport/Legends|viewport]], causing him to crash.<ref name="agent" />

The team returned to the now-abandoned homestead, and with WeeGee's help they recovered the digital map from 8t88's head. In celebration he started dancing with Jan, which led to hugging and kissing, and confessing their love for each other. They were joined by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] who sought news about their quest for the Valley. It was then when Kyle realised that Jan knew his personal quest all along, had "borrowed" the holodisc, and was there to spy on him; but Jan convinced him that she had no other choice, given his overall stance of secrecy. Luke welcomed him to the Jedi Knighthood, as his training was complete, and confirmed that he had heard the voice of Morgan.<ref name="agent" />

====The way to Ruusan====
[[File:Kyleincockpit.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Katarn during the [[Battle in the Milagro system]].]]
The team departed Sulon for the ''New Hope'', stationed over [[Milagro/Legends|Milagro]]; however as they exited [[hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in [[Milagro system/Legends|the system]], it was near a damaged Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] and her escort, while she was launching [[TIE_Series|TIEs]] against the dreadnaught.<ref name="JK" /> Luke suggested to engage them in order to give to the ''Hope'' some time until reinforcements would arrive, to which Jan objected, believing they would not survive more than a couple of [[standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. Kyle, who was her co-pilot, encouraged her to trust the feelings of a Jedi,<ref name="JKaudio">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref> and also suggested to enter the defensive sphere around the Star Destroyer, as the craft would avoid firing and hitting each other. The strategy proved to be right, as the Imperial Captain [[Purdy M. Trico]], in his wrath, ordered to break formation in order to be able to destroy the two ships, thus losing its defenses.<ref name="JK" />

Kyle fought in the [[battle in the Milagro system]] as Jan's co-pilot next to Skywalker, who, guided by the Force, guided them between laser fire; they were joined by three [[squadron/Legends|squadrons]] led by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' who took advantage of the opening in the Destroyer's defenses.<ref name="JK" /> When the ''Falcon'' was overwhelmed by six fighters, Kyle helped Solo by hitting one. The two exchanged a few remarks, joking that the Alliance had to make do with cargo freighters participating in battles.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

The outcome of the battle was victorious, and the ''Crow'' made it back to the ''Hope''. On their way to Mon Mothma's cabin Kyle was surprised that, for the first time, he was not required to surrender his arms by the sentries before entering. Jan guessed his thoughts and they exchanged a smile.<ref name="JK" /> Kyle met in person [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and Han Solo, to whom, at the request of Mon Mothma, explained the background concerning Jerec and the Valley, and narrated his adventure. Mon Mothma explained to Solo that Jan and Kyle were those who stole the Death Star plans in the first place, and admitted that Jan, Luke and Leia convinced her to trust Kyle to pursue his quest; Solo related to him, as he was still not trusted by the Command. Kyle intended to go to Ruusan with Jan and stop Jerec. Luke affirmed that if he could release the Jedi spirits, Jerec would have no powers from them, countering Mothma's skepticism, that a fully equipped battle group would be necessary to confront him. Kyle affirmed that this mission was exactly what his fate was always guiding him to all along, even without her support.<ref name="JKaudio" /><ref name="JK" />

[[File:KyleMeetsLuke-DF3.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Katarn meeting [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] for the first time.]]
Concerning the question of passing Jerec's blockade, Kyle picked on Han's thought and suggested hiding the ''Crow'' in one of the captured Imperial transports; Han and Jan added that the transport should deliver ammunitions, such as sabotaged [[torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]].<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" /> Kyle and Jan descended to Milagro and [[Military Correction Facility 63]], where they interviewed [[prison/Legends|prison]]er [[Alfonso Luiz Obota]], and seeing his suitability, they offered him the job for the mission.<ref name="JK" />

According to the [[Mission to Ruusan|mission plan]], a Rebel task force posed as the crew of the Imperial freighter ''[[High Hauler]]'', which, after a Rebel attack, retreated to [[Ruusan system/Legends|Hoth's Brand system]]. Obota posed as the acting Captain while Kyle was disguised as an Imperial [[Warrant Officer/Legends|Warrant Officer]].<ref name="JK" /> Kyle reported to Obota their arrival and the readings of the [[scanner/Legends|scanner]], and later they congratulated Obota for his acting. He notified Obota when the Imperial inspection team disembarked at the docking bay.<ref name="JKaudio" /> The agents acted their roles through the inspection, and when [[Unidentified_Imperial_lieutenant_(Ruusan)|the Imperial Lieutenant]] started questioning the ''Crow'' on the ''Hauler''{{'s}} hangar, he interrupted the discussion. For the following hours, the ''Moldy Crow'' was cleared for some routine serving missions such as supply runs, allowing Kyle and Jan to gather intelligence and familiarise with the planet.<ref name="JK" />

====Race to the Valley====
[[File:Moldy_Crow_Nowhere_ruins.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Kyle and Jan flying above the ruins of [[Fort Nowhere]].]]
Once the ''Hauler''{{'s}} mission was completed, the ''Moldy Crow'' was launched above Ruusan's [[atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. They flew towards a [[Fort Nowhere|settlement]] that [[battle of Fort Nowhere|was attacked]] recently by the Imperials, in hopes of finding some lead.<ref name="JK" /> On their way, Kyle again heard Rahn's voice, warning him not to give in to hate; Jan realised it, but Kyle didn't mention what he heard.<ref name="JKaudio" /> Kyle picked up a familiar signal using a [[primitive binary code]] used years ago by his father.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" /> which pointed to a certain location. Jan questioned this route, and Kyle ensured her that it there were no Imperials there. They flew above the ruins of the Fort, and Kyle failed to sense any sentient life there. They reached the location told by the signal, Kyle ensuring Jan that they were observed, but could not say who they were. Kyle touched the gravel and unearthed an ancient dagger blade, and Jan also found a helmet, the site of an ancient battlefield. The sound of a [[Fido|drone]] interrupted their thoughts, and Kyle prevented her from unholstering her blaster. The [[Flier|flyer]] inspected them and then beckoned them to the west. They took again the ''Crow'' and followed it, until they reached a an [[unidentified Ruusan temple|ancient temple]]; the hideout of the settlers.<ref name="JK" />

There Kyle was reunited with a Sulonese, [[Grif Grawley]], who knew him in his youth. He had followed his father to this colony in order to escape the Empire. It was he who guided them to the temple. Knowing Kyle's Imperial background, he questioned him and demanded to surrender their weapons;<ref name="JK" /> Kyle advised Jan to comply.<ref name="JKaudio" /> They retreated to the temple and exchanged their news, about the colonization, the Imperial attack and flight in the wastelands; Kyle talked about his father's murder and how he defected to the Alliance. [[Luther Pardy]], who was holding Kyle's weapons, was unconvinced about Kyle's honesty and mentions about the Jedi and accused him of being an Imperial spy. As a response Kyle Force pulled his lightsaber from Luther's belt and ignited it; this convinced Grif and others about his claims.<ref name="JK" />

That night, Grif with a team of the colonists led Kyle outside in order to meet the native species, the [[Bouncer]]s, who, as Grif learned later, they had met Morgan and acknowledged him a "knight". Kyle was amazed thinking that his father perhaps chose not to become a Jedi Knight even though he could. Right when they saw a group of Bouncers, Kyle felt another presence: an [[Patrol_341|Imperial patrol]] came only to attack the indigenous creatures without reason. Jan wanted to warn them but it was already too late. Kyle saw Jan arming herself with a [[blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] to save the defenseless natives;<ref name="JK" /> In vain he tried to dissuade her, pointing out that it was not part of their mission and their presence would be exposed to the Imperials.<ref name="JKaudio" /> Kyle just told Grif it was an opportunity for retribution; but should not allow anyone to leave alive. Indeed, after Jan hit a [[speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]], the Imperials stopped to look for them; the [[skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] rounded them and started firing, causing rocks to amputate and then kill a Rebel close to Kyle. Grif and Kyle and most of the group moved towards the squad of Stormtroopers, sneaked behind them and Kyle made short work of them with his lightsaber, including [[Aagon|their leader]].<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

They spent the rest of the night burying the dead in carefully made graves and collect all equipment and scrap from the battle that could potentially point to their involvement in the patrol's disappearance, which Kyle found very tedious to do in the dark. Weapons and equipment were loaded on the skimmer. At sunrise Kyle insisted to look for more, but Grawley believed that the area was too big and they had to leave before dawn.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

[[File:Kyleandbouncers.jpg|thumb|200px|The [[Bouncer]] leader communicating with Kyle Katarn.]]
One of the Bouncers, whom Grawley referred to as [[Floater (Bouncer)|Floater]], agreed to serve as a guide to the Valley for Katarn. Kyle thanked [[Unidentified Bouncer leader|their leader]], advised him to hide, and exchanged condolences as both groups lost one of them. The Jedi asked the Bouncer leader if he knew if they would succeed in stopping Jerec, but the Bouncer could not be reassuring and just replied by writing down a line from the same poem they had told to his father: Kyle or someone else, sooner or later, would come to fight a battle and free the spirits trapped in the Valley.<ref name="JK" /> The pair went out but Kyle delayed going to Valley and decided to return to the Temple. Jan did not understand why, and the only thing Kyle could explain was that the time was not right for it; they should wait in the Temple because something was about to change.<ref name="JKaudio" />

It was first after a long time the couple had time to sleep, but in the afternoon Grif warned him that a patrol was approaching. Kyle marveled at Jan's sleeping posture but had to wake her up and showed some lust as she put on her pants. Grif had prepared the [[Zeta 19|captured skimmer]] in order to lead the patrol away from their base and have the leisure to spy on them. Kyle was refused to take [[Floater]] with them as it would be too dangerous for him. After some time, the skimmer brought them to a mesa. It took them half an hour to reach the top, and Kyle envied the older man for keeping his breath while Kyle's was out.<ref name="JK" /> From the top of the mesa they spied to the Imperials through electrobinoculars, as they approached the rocky area where the skirmish occurred and they buried the bodies, worrying that their presence would be discovered. Not only that, but Kyle understood that among them was a Dark Jedi. It was Yun, who had also sensed their presence, and Kyle's, from the distance, but it was when he had a change of heart and decided to repay his debt. Jan wondered why the Jedi, knowing they are there, hadn't summoned their forces to destroy them. Kyle could not explain but ensured her that they would be safe for the remainder of the night and should proceed with their mission.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

That night, the team flew into a canyon, as close to Valley as possible. Kyle left WeeGee in the ''Crow'' and with the help of [[Night-vision goggles/Legends|night goggles]], they followed Floater's guidance among the mazelike canyons. They had to rappel down or ascend vertical cliffs and slopes in order to cover the five-kilometer distance without being spotted by Jerec's forces. Not far from their destination, Kyle was climbing a rope below Grif, who reached a flat shelf and was spotted by [[attack droid]] [[AD-43]]. Kyle could only watch as the older man jumped on the droid wrestling with his strength. The droid hovered around as a wounded Grif attempted to damage it with his hunting knife, until its guidance system threw them to a rocky wall, and they fell together into the canyon. Grif's death was felt by Kyle through the Force.<ref name="JK" />

Besides the sad event, the two considered that the droid might had alerted the Imperials, forcing them either to go back, or proceed before they could reinforce their perimeter. They decided to follow Floater who continued high above them, and they managed to remain unseen by shuttles and speeder bike patrols that passed nearby. Floater led them to an ancient, enclosed aqueduct which followed the land contour, and led directly to the Valley. Before entering, Kyle gave to Floater an approving pat. Equipped with glow rods, they followed Floater and emerged crawling to the other side.<ref name="JK" /> Near the Valley now, Kyle could feel years of waiting in pain like thousands of tortured voices. Floater reminded that the last part of the prophecy remained: a battle to be fought.<ref name="JKaudio" /> The view of the Imperial garrison built by Jerec around the Valley was a sight much worse than Kyle had anticipated. With their electrobinoculars, they spotted Jerec standing on a platform of the tall central tower with a woman, Sariss. He then decided to make his way to the tower, insisting that Jan should remain behind and not leave Floater alone.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

By dawn Kyle made it to the bottom by descending a three-sided chimney, and once on the ground he hid behind rocks. The area until the base of the tower was open and he had to wait for an hour until an opportunity showed up. All this time he had a sense of uneasiness, but when he tried to reach out the Force, he found no danger; he didn't know that they had been observed and Jan was Jerec's target. Then a [[Tractor/Legends|tractor]] passed in front of him, followed by a [[train/Legends|train]] of fifteen cars. Using the raising cloud of dust, and high noise as a cover, he darted towards the tractor and hid into a coupler; at the right moment, he dashed out and sprinted towards one of the tower's footings. The agent marched to the [[turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] at the inner core, while the sentries had their backs towards him.<ref name="JK" /> He decided to assume the role of an Imperial so as not to attract any attention, until the watch command questioned him; the Jedi, with the lightsaber in view, gave an aura of authority and mentioned Jerec's name, making the guard to apologize and leave.<ref name="JKaudio" /> A brace of Commandos emerged from the lift, and Kyle understood that they mistook him for a Dark Jedi; courtesy of the lightsaber tucked under his arm. Kyle took a frowning strict expression, until they left and rode the lift up.<ref name="JK" />

====Choosing sides====
[[File:Maw_kyle.jpg|thumb|200px|Maw in a fierce duel with Kyle Katarn]]
Preparing to confront Jerec, his mind was full of worries, which were answered once more by Rahn; the voice ensured the Jedi that Rahn, and the Force, were still with Kyle, and that his lack of overconfidence was a sign of strength.<ref name="JKaudio" /><ref name="JK" /> He advised not to give in to hate,<ref name="JKaudio" /> and to mention Rahn's name against the "half-man" who was waiting for Kyle. Soon the turbolift stopped. A [[Courier droid|messenger droid]] entered, and Kyle exited to an empty platform wondering who the "half-man" was. Then his Force sense returned after its recent absence,<ref name="JK" /> and on the nearby landing deck he saw the ''Moldy Crow'', realizing that it had been captured; his thoughts were cut when he was pushed by something that sent him down. He ignited his saber and saw [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]],<ref name="JKaudio" /> a Dark Jedi whom Rahn had cut in half; his torso was alive and floating thanks to the Force. Maw's eyes were full of hatred, and he became full of wrath when Kyle mentioned Rahn's name. Maw charged with great speed,<ref name="JK" /> and they exchanged several strikes<ref name="JKaudio" /><ref name="dfjk" /> but the Kyle fell back down, and injured Maw severely with his lightsaber as he passed above; Maw dropped his lightsaber and fell onto the walkway.<ref name="JK" /> Kyle approached him and Maw taunted Katarn to kill him unarmed, lying how his father whimpered on his knees when he was killed; and that it was he who had placed Morgan's head on a pike for all to see. The thought that killing a murdering brute would bring justice passed from Kyle's mind, who, without even realizing it, acquiesced to his anger and cut Maw's torso [[Sai tok/Legends|in two]]; he realized in shock what he did, when he felt dark energy swirling around him.<ref name="JK" /><ref name="JKaudio" />

[[File:Kyle_katarn1.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Katarn after slaying [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]]]]
Just then he realised that everything was staged; Jerec emerged from the same turbolift, leading Sariss and [[Boc Aseca|Boc]], with a captured and bound Jan Ors. He congratulated Katarn for his first step towards the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark Side]], and offered a place at his side if he would strike Ors down. Katarn indeed felt the temptation for ambition, to the point of hesitating, only to feel disgusted with himself moments later. He refused, staying true to his father. Disappointed but not surprised, Jerec blasted Katarn with dark energy, damaging parts of the outpost and trapping Katarn inside the ''Sulon Star''. Katarn made his way through the tumbling vessel and attempted to reach the airlock, where his ship, the ''Moldy Crow'', was docked.<ref name="JK" />

Activating the emergency start-up, he released the ship without the safety procedures. The ''Crow'' escaped the falling ship but was unable to gain height and one of her wings smashed against a rock spire. Upon hitting the planet's surface, Katarn, who wasn't strapped on the pilot seat, hit the console and was rendered unconscious. Once more he joined the Force and saw a vision of Rahn who showed him how "that which ''was''" became "that which ''is''"; in his dream, Kyle [[Force-bond/Legends|bonded]] with the ancient Jedi Master [[Tal (Jedi)|Tal]] of the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]]; he also experienced the actions of Dark Jedi [[LaTor]], one of those of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] who created the [[thought bomb]] with Kaan, and the massacre that happened at that very place a [[millennium/Legends|millennium]] ago, killing all of them.<ref name="JK" />

Meanwhile the three remaining Dark Jedi were sent by Jerec to find and kill him. They found the unconscious pilot in the wreckage of the ''Crow'' and pulled him outside. It was night when Kyle opened his eyes, full of pain. [[Boc Aseca]], the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Dark Jedi sadistically teased and taunted him, holding Rahn's lightsaber hilt before him. Seeing the Dark Jedi raising a rock above it, Kyle in vain attempted to rise and stop him from destroying the lightsaber, leaving him defenseless. Sariss was ready to execute Katarn, and sent the Twi'lek to report it to Jerec. But Yun, out of sympathy for Katarn, who had previously spared his life and feeling guilty for executing so many people, blocked her blow with [[Yun's lightsaber|his yellow-bladed lightsaber]]. Sariss' blade was deflected mortally wounding Yun. As Katarn recovered his strength, he heard the young man's final words, possibly redeeming himself, stating that as a Jedi, Kyle deserved a battle.<ref name="dfjk" />
[[File:Kyle_sariss.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Sariss dueling Kyle Katarn near the wrecked ''Moldy Crow''.]]
Katarn picked up Yun's lightsaber and called upon Tal's wisdom. With his help he fended off a [[Alter Mind|mental attack]] and attacked back. The two dueled, surprising Sariss with his knowledge and skill. When Sariss started wearing him out, wounding his face, Kyle realized that it should end quick. When Sariss lunged, he parried; they were blade-to-blade Kyle used Tal's [[Flowing Water]] Cut; as Sariss withdrew he "expanded" with his mind and body, and the saber penetrated Sariss' chest.<ref name="JK" />

Katarn defeated Sariss<ref name="dfjk" /> as [[Hoth's Brand|the sun]] was rising, forming a shadow of a stone pinnacle to point at the Valley below; Kyle knew that this was his destination. He saluted dead Yun and rushed there. He used [[Mind trick/Legends|Mind trick]] to get past some sentries and a patrol and followed the stairs down. At the floor of the chamber, he was attracted to a glint, and moving closer he saw it was the [[Multi-tool/Legends|multitool]] of his father, which Kyle had given to him years earlier with a dedication. The memory of his father, and the knowledge he had made it that far, gave him strength.<ref name="JK" />

====Bringing of justice====
[[File:Kyle_boc.jpg|thumb|200px|Boc facing Katarn in a fierce duel]]
An arch marked the entrance to the Valley of the Jedi which Kyle explored; he saw effigies of Lord Hoth and Tal. He climbed a tall tomb, and from the elevated location he spotted Jan tied to a column.

He rescued Ors. Disguising himself as one of the statues that littered the Valley, Boc revealed himself and attacked Katarn. Boc wielded [[Jar'Kai/Legends|two lightsabers]] against which the self-taught Jedi Knight attempted to cope, both with his fencing skills and Tal's knowledge. Boc's flurrying sabers immobilized Kyle, until Rahn reminded him that his mind is also a weapon; Ors supported Kyle by throwing rocks at Boc, distracting the Twi'lek, giving to Kyle some time to concentrate. Giving way to the dark side of the Force, Katarn struck Boc with an ancient dark side technique known as [[Darkshear|spear of midnight black]].<ref name="JK" /> Immediately, Katarn feared he was lost to the darkness, and tried to remember how many steps it took to follow the dark path forever. It was then that Katarn realized he was truly a Jedi; it doesn't matter how far down you follow the dark path once you have returned from it.<ref name="dfjk" />

[[File:Duel_at_the_valley_of_the_jedi.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kyle Katarn faces an empowered Jerec.]]
After killing Boc, Katarn had defeated all of Jerec's Dark Jedi. Katarn then descended into the Valley Core to face the Dark Master himself. The two dueled, and though the Dark Jedi Jerec had already begun drawing power from the Valley of the Jedi and used its power to heal himself, Katarn was able to defeat him with a light-side technique that disrupted Jerec's connection to the Force. Disarmed, Jerec tried to turn Katarn to the dark side by reminding him of his father's murder, urging Katarn to strike him down and claim the Valley's dark power for himself. Katarn, not wishing to feed his hatred, showed Jerec mercy and tossed the Dark Jedi his saber. Jerec would not accept Katarn's mercy and once again attacked him. Katarn's [[Falling Leaf|counterstrike]] killed Jerec, and he fulfilled the prophecy by freeing the souls of the countless Jedi trapped by Lord [[Skere Kaan]] for over a thousand years. Afterwards, Katarn carved statues of Rahn and his father, and thanked the spirit of his father.<ref name="JK" />

====Fall to the dark side====
{{Quote|Master Kyle Katarn succumbed to it. He was brought back to the light side of the Force only by his pupil.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref>}}
Katarn continued working with Ors for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], cooperating with [[Bey]], [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] and [[Guri]] on an insertion mission to the [[Tof/Legends|Tof]] fortress world [[Saijo/Legends|Saijo]], and nearly razing [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]] while apprehending the [[Ketton/Legends|Ketton]] spy [[Derrida/Legends|Derrida]] with the help of a [[Max (bunny)|pint-sized lagomorph]]. Katarn took a more permanent role with the New Republic and served with Lieutenant [[Judder Page/Legends|Judder Page's]] [[Katarn Commandos]], executing missions on various worlds like [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], [[Boudolayz]] and [[Garos IV]].<ref name="Saga1" />

[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] offered to train Katarn further, but Katarn declined, still holding a great fear of the dark side.<ref name="Saga1" /><ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

[[File:Training.jpg|thumb|243x243px|Katarn training Mara Jade]]
At the height of the [[Imperial Civil War]], a clone of Palpatine arranged for the capture of the former Imperial assassin [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], who had left the service of the Empire and cut herself off from the Emperor's telepathic commands after killing a [[Luuke Skywalker|clone]] of Luke Skywalker in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. After Jade was intercepted on a mission to [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Cronal]] had her imprisoned, but she was eventually rescued by New Republic forces under the command of Kyle Katarn.<ref name="DFS5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}}</ref> Mara later sought out Katarn<ref name="MOTS" /> and persuaded him to train her in the ways of the Force, taking up the rare bond of [[reciprocal apprenticeship]], a facet of the [[Concordance of Fealty]] little practiced since the [[Cleansing of the Nine Houses]].<ref name="Saga1" /> During this period, the pair moved to [[Altyr V]] to support a New Republic base there. While sabotaging an Imperial [[Battle of Altyr V (Galactic Civil War)|assault]], Katarn discovered a lead to the Sith ruins on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], a long dead world. Katarn investigated the ruins but came under the influence of the dark side of the Force. His former pupil Mara Jade went to him and [[Mission to Dromund Kaas (New Republic)|dueled]] him. Seeing that combat was futile, she lowered her guard and opened herself to Katarn's killing blow. Just as Katarn's blade was about to touch Jade's neck, he stopped in remorse for his actions, turning back to the light side, for the time.<ref name="MOTS" /><ref name="Saga1" />

===Training under Luke Skywalker===
[[File:KyleKatarn-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Katarn accepted Skywalker's offer for training at the Jedi Praxeum.]]Skywalker made a second offer to train Katarn when he founded the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and Katarn accepted, now convinced that it would help him tame his dark side.<ref name="Saga1" /> In that time he put Yun's lightsaber away and built [[Kyle Katarn's third lightsaber|his own blue-bladed lightsaber as part of his training.]]<ref name="Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast">The saber that Katarn turned over to Skywalker and received back in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'', denoting that he had stopped using Yun's saber.</ref> Katarn, once again a Jedi Knight, developed a friendship with fellow Jedi trainee [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], but when two students of the Academy fell to the dark side, Katarn's fear of succumbing multiplied. Katarn gave up the Force, entrusting his lightsaber to Skywalker, and returned to his mercenary ways as Jan Ors' partner, taking on unofficial missions for the New Republic.<ref name="Saga1" /><ref name="Saga2" />

===Missions for the New Republic===
During the next year, Katarn was noted several times by [[New Republic Intelligence Service|NRI]] investigators for his overzealous attitude in eliminating Imperial Remnant soldiers. He was sent on a [[Mission to Tatooine (Kyle Katarn)|mission]] to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in order to roust Imperial recruiters, during which he saw that they were looking for Force sensitives. Despite being an intelligence-gathering mission, he followed his trigger-happy tactic and raided the Imperial headquarters; an Imperial, allegedly the ringleader, talked about "reborn", before being killed in the encounter. In his report to Mon Mothma, Katarn noted that the opposite forces were heavier than expected and requested a corresponding raise to his money; but he also downplayed the Imperial casualties. He also expressed the wish that it would be the last mission dealing with the Force.<ref name="N.R.I. Reports">{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}}</ref>

He had only left one Imperial survivor for questioning who was so distressed from his ordeal that he could barely relate what little information he had while under interrogation. All that the interrogation staff could get him to say was that his orders had come from [[Kejim]].<ref name="jk2" />

====The Empire Reborn====
=====Kejim=====
Katarn was sent to the world of Kejim to act on this information and en route, fragments of Remnant communication intercepted by the New Republic were relayed to Katarn and Ors, which further affirmed the Republic's suspicions that something was going on. Katarn was at first hesitant to go; the transmission had made mention of the Valley of the Jedi. There was indeed a Remnant base on the planet where, upon a detailed inspection of the base, Katarn found [[Artusian crystal|strange crystals]] which, when analyzed, were found to be very similar to [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|ones used in a Jedi's lightsaber]]. Their mission would have ended there, as Katarn didn't want to get involved with anything that involved the Force, but Ors accepted the job on his behalf—having been offered twice their usual fee by Mon Mothma.<ref name="jk2" />

=====Artus Prime=====
[[File:Kylevalley.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Katarn reconnecting with the Force in the Force nexus of the Valley of the Jedi.]]
It was soon revealed that the [[Empire Reborn]] movement<ref name="dfs">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}}</ref> under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]] was using the planet to experiment with these crystals and the Force, particularly in infusing non-Force-sensitives with Force potential. Although Katarn successfully infiltrated and shut down the outpost, Ors was later [[Battle of Artus Prime|captured]] and apparently killed by the Dark Jedi [[Desann]] and his student [[Tavion Axmis]] on the planet [[Artus Prime]].<ref name="jk2" />

=====Returning to the Valley of the Jedi and reobtaining his lightsaber=====
Angered by his partner's "death," Katarn regained his Force powers by using the power of the Valley of the Jedi, once again treading dangerously close to the dark side. He went to the [[Jedi Academy]] to retrieve his blue-bladed lightsaber he had left with Skywalker, where he then learned that Desann was a fallen student of Skywalker's. Katarn was forced to work again with his old Master,<ref name="Saga1" /> and set out with Skywalker across the galaxy to bring Desann to justice.

=====Nar Shaddaa and Bespin=====
[[File:Kyle_Katarn_Stormtroopers.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Kyle Katarn fighting a group of stormtroopers.]]
While Skywalker [[Skirmish at the Valley of the Jedi|secretly]] went to the Valley of the Jedi to look for Desann, Katarn tracked Desann's shuttle to [[Reelo Baruk]] on Nar Shaddaa; there he encountered Lando Calrissian, who helped Katarn escape Nar Shaddaa and kill Baruk in the process. Still, Katarn became haunted with the memories of hearing Desann's order to Axmis to "murder" Ors and her screams. These only served to make him desire revenge even more. He eventually got the chance to exact his revenge at [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], where he encountered Desann's apprentice, Tavion. She informed Katarn of his blunder in revealing the location of the Valley to herself and her master, and was defeated by Katarn in lightsaber combat. Katarn used [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force grip]] to lift Axmis over a Cloud City platform and held her there. She confessed to everything, revealing that Ors was still alive. Katarn spared Axmis's life and tossed her aside.<ref name="jk2" /> Axmis ran away, only to plot her revenge on Katarn for sparing her.<ref name="ja" />

=====Infiltrating the ''Doomgiver''=====
Kyle then took a hauler to the [[Cairn Installation]], where the ''[[Doomgiver]]'', the ship on which Ors was being held captive, was docked. Katarn ran into Skywalker again, and learned of the purpose behind the crystals he had discovered on Kejim, and of Desann's troops being in the Valley of the Jedi. Katarn infiltrated the ''Doomgiver'' as it launched into hyperspace and successfully found Ors and released her. Ors informed Katarn of Desann's fascination with the Academy on Yavin IV. Katarn realized where the ''Doomgiver'' was headed and with Ors's help, he was able to shut down the ''Doomgiver''{{'s}} shield generator, allowing [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] to destroy the vessel. At the same time, Katarn was able to prevent the mad [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], Galak Fyyar, from assuming the throne as "Emperor Galak Fyyar the first."<ref name="jk2" />

[[File:KylevsDesann.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kyle Katarn duels an enraged Desann.]]
As the ''Doomgiver'' exploded, Katarn and Ors escaped to Yavin IV. After separating, with a kiss, to assist the Jedi Academy, Katarn fought through the jungles of Yavin IV against the legions of new Shadowtroopers and Reborn. Katarn eventually made it into the Academy itself in time to fend off an invading force of stormtroopers. He descended further into the academy gallantly fighting off dozens of reborn and Shadowtroopers, while rescuing many Jedi in the process. Katarn continued into the bowels of the ruins, where he faced off with Desann. Both Desann and Katarn attempted to persuade the other to change sides, but Desann soon attacked Katarn out of frustration. Katarn defeated the Dark Jedi, ending his plan for domination.
