Skywalker thanked Katarn for his assistance and offered him a place at the Academy. Katarn declined, at least for the moment. Katarn joined the Academy later, after he and Ors spent time together on the beaches of [[Spira/Legends|Spira]].<ref name="jk2" />

====Other adventures====
Shortly thereafter the Academy was attacked by a [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] and her band of outlaws who were seeking ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] artifacts owned by the Academy. Despite the fact that the Nightsisters took advantage of the Academy's weakened state, Katarn and a group of others managed to push them back and kept them from retrieving the artifacts.<ref name="AoE">{{SWMiniCite|set=Attack on Endor Scenario Pack}}</ref>

Sometime later, Katarn and Ors were sent to investigate Remnant activity on [[Alzoc III/Legends|Alzoc III]]. Katarn infiltrated the base by hiding in a shipping crate and proceeded with his mission. Meanwhile, Ors was discovered by a squad of stormtroopers in one of the hangars, and was taken hostage at blasterpoint. Katarn managed to rescue her, but not before killing two [[Reborn]], who had survived Desann's defeat. Together, Katarn and Ors escaped on the ''[[Raven's Claw]]''.<ref name="JKII demo">[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast demo|''Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'' demo]]</ref>

===The New Jedi Order===
====Instructor====
{{Quote|Master Katarn, it's an honor to be serving under you.''"<br />"''First off, just call me Kyle – titles make my skin crawl. Secondly, you're not 'serving under me'. I'm gonna teach you, and you're gonna learn. That's it.|Rosh Penin's first encounter with Katarn|<ref name="ja" />}}
[[File:Korr_Katarn_Penin.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Katarn with his two apprentices, [[Jaden Korr|Korr]] and [[Rosh Penin|Penin]].]]
With the victory over Desann's Dark Jedi, Katarn renewed his confidence and returned to Skywalker and was promoted to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and continued in the ways of the Force as an instructor at Skywalker's Academy.<ref name="Saga1" /><ref name="negc" />

Katarn took on two students, [[Jaden Korr]] and [[Rosh Penin]] to train in the ways of the Jedi. Both students were talented, progressing quickly under Katarn's tutelage. When a new threat, a Dark Jedi cult known as the [[Disciples of Ragnos]], began threatening the galaxy, Katarn and his apprentices investigated numerous worlds on the trail of the Disciples. Their investigations spanned the galaxy, from [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] worlds such as [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] to [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territory]] worlds such as [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]].<ref name="ja" /> Katarn submitted numerous [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] reports to the Jedi on their success, including [[Tauntaun Feathers A1]] from [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].<ref name="hui">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031204hoth|text=Hoth: Under the Ice|int=Hoth: Under the Ice}}</ref>

The Jedi Order discovered that the leader of the Cult was none other than Tavion Axmis,<ref name="ja" /> the former apprentice of Desann.<ref name="jk2" /> Katarn's investigations revealed she had acquired the [[Scepter of Ragnos]] from [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]] and planned to use it to resurrect the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Marka Ragnos]]. While Katarn was away, his apprentice, Penin, was kidnapped and eventually succumbed to the dark side, joining the Disciples of Ragnos.<ref name="ja" />

[[File:Kyle_Katarn_NR.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Kyle Katarn as a member of the New Jedi Order.]]
Katarn's other student, Jaden, joined with Katarn to investigate the planet of Vjun, the site of Darth Vader's castle. There, the two were forced to split up when Katarn's perch dropped out from beneath him and he fell down several floors. Korr, reaching the top of the building alone, faced off against the fallen Penin. Korr pleaded with his former friend to return to the light side, but was unsuccessful. Killing the [[Kothos twins|Reborn Twins]], [[Vil Kothos|Vil]] and [[Dasariah Kothos]], Korr overcame Penin and closed in on him. Katarn then rejoined him at the top of the tower. Suddenly, Tavion Axmis emerged from behind a pillar and stated her role as the leader of the cult. Subduing Korr and Katarn with Force lightning, she stated that Katarn should have killed her when he had the chance. Using the Force to pull Jaden's saber from his grasp, she used it to bring down the roof, and she escaped with Penin. Korr's actions on Vjun and Katarn's recommendation saw to Skywalker promoting Jaden Kor to Jedi Knight.<ref name="ja" />

Katarn later received a transmission from Penin pleading for help. Given his own experience of redemption at the hands of Mara Jade, Katarn was determined to answer the call. Requesting Korr to join him, the pair set out once more, this time to [[Taspir III]]. Once again separated, Korr went on ahead and confronted Penin. Urged on by [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|Alora]], he considered killing Penin as retribution. In the facilities below, Katarn sensed his precarious position and telepathically urged Jaden to spare Penin's life. Following his master's wishes, Jaden spared Penin's life and fought Alora, who severed Penin's left arm. Jaden killed Alora just as Katarn arrived. Katarn ordered Jaden to aid the Jedi on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], as he rushed Penin off to a medical facility.<ref name="ja" />

Katarn went on to become the Academy's foremost [[Battlemaster/Legends|Jedi Battlemaster]], educating many other Knights, and fighting in the [[Third Battle of Yavin]] where he stopped and killed a rampaging former [[Emperor's Hand]] by the name of [[Jeng Droga]].<ref name="Saga1" />

====Yuuzhan Vong War====
{{Quote|You chose to intervene. Your choice saved these people.|Kyle Katarn to Lieutenant Pallin|<ref name="eao">{{Tales|21|Equals & Opposites}}</ref>}}
[[File:Katarnnjo.jpg|thumb|Kyle Katarn revealing himself to the Yuuzhan Vong.]]
During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Katarn helped develop strategies against the invaders and freed the Human captives of the Imperial Remnant world [[Ord Sedra]],<ref name="Saga1" /> During a meeting with the Imperial leadership, Kyle and Jan received a message from Imperial [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Pallin]] seeking for help, reporting a transport belonging to the [[Peace Brigade]]. The couple diverted to the planet and cooperated with Pallin, with Kyle posing as one of the slaves. While in the column herded by the members of the Brigade, he decided to break his disguise when he stopped to help a young girl. The surprise and Kyle's Force powers allowed Pallin's Stormtroopers to immobilize the invaders and their collaborators.<ref name="eao" /> 

After their success, Katarn once more proposed marriage to Jan Ors, but she rejected him on the basis of not wanting to get married only because of fear that every day might be their last. Their private moment was interrupted when the Vong escaped from their captivity not long after, the Imperials failed to contain them in a small security building. Jan jumped into action leaving Kyle behind, and Pallin directed him inside the building. Kyle fought a Yuuzhan Vong warlord who put Jan inside a cell. Kyle killed him with a bomb while Jan saved herself with his lightsaber. Bragging about his heroic rescue, he repeated his proposal, only to be turned down once more.<ref name="eao" />

Near the end of the war, the living planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] agreed to help the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], and Katarn was one of several Jedi Knights bonded to seed-partners and provided with Sekotan starships in the final battle.<ref name="tuf" /><ref name="Saga1" />

After the end of the invasion, Katarn heard a rumor that the Sith still existed. Due to his personal experience on Dromund Kaas, Katarn investigated, following clues from Ord Sedra in the [[Clacis sector/Legends|Clacis sector]] first to [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]], and then halfway across the galaxy to the [[Cloak of the Sith/Legends|Cloak of the Sith]]. Katarn went missing there; his fate resembling that of [[Daye Azur-Jamin]], who had disappeared earlier in the Yuuzhan Vong war.<ref name="Saga1" />

It turned out that indeed Katarn had been kidnapped by Azur-Jamin, who had meanwhile turned to the dark side. Mara Jade Skywalker, Jaden Korr and a group of [[Alpha Blue]] specialists led by Jan Ors headed to the [[Cloak of the Sith/Legends|Cloak of the Sith]] region, finding Katarn and freeing him from Azur-Jamin, who revealed the presence of a Force-sensitive [[Vongerella|Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="Saga2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}}</ref> Katarn returned safely, and took an active role in Jedi affairs, being appointed to the [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Masters' council]].<ref name="dn1" />

====Jedi Council Member====
[[File:OctaKyle.jpg|thumb|left|190px|Katarn on [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]]]]
Five years after the war with the Yuuzhan Vong, Katarn had become a respected Master not afraid to voice his opinion. During the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], along with Han, Leia, and [[Kyp Durron]] Katarn traveled to the moon of [[Kr]] where the [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] Dark Nest [[Gorog]] dwelt, coordinating the conflict. Katarn joined the [[Battle of Kr|fighting on the ground]] while the Solos helped Luke and Mara Skywalker.<ref name="dn1" /> As the Killik crisis continued to expand, Katarn later led a Stealth-X squadron of Jedi Knights against the Dark Nest.<ref name="dn2">''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]''</ref>

At some point during his service to the New Jedi Order, he wrote the ''[[Spy Primer]]'', a guide for espionage agents. [[Jae Juun]] and [[Tarfang]] were known to have read it as part of their training.<ref name="dn3">''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]''</ref>

====Second Galactic Civil War====
Four years after the Killik crisis, Katarn remained on the council. As the tensions grew between [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and the Galactic Alliance, Luke Skywalker's nephew [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], under the sway of the dark side, began interning, [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] and executing Corellians under his new role as a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]] in the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]]. Katarn voiced his concerns along with fellow Council-member Cilghal to Skywalker that Solo was a dangerous force in his new capacity.<ref name="bloodlines">''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]''</ref> As the conflict progressed, eventually developing into the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Katarn also suggested that Leia Organa Solo be exiled due to her actions which appeared contrary to the wishes of the government he continued to serve. However, Katarn was only acting as a voice of moderation, and came to realize that the Jedi sometimes had to act beyond the government.<ref name="exile">''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''</ref>

Katarn's fears of Solo were justified as the wayward Jedi Knight amassed a wealth of allies and political power. Eventually, Solo modified the laws governing the Galactic Alliance and deposed Chief of State [[Cal Omas]] in a ''coup d'etat''. Following the mysterious death of Mara Jade Skywalker, his former apprentice, Katarn attended the [[Funeral of Mara Jade Skywalker|funeral]] of his close friend. When Solo, who was, unknown to Katarn, now Darth Caedus, arrived at the funeral, Katarn, along with Kyp Durron and [[Kenth Hamner]], told the co-Chief of State that he was unwelcome to stand with the Jedi Masters, and that his place was either with the Knights, or the visiting dignitaries; however, a grieving Grand Master Skywalker overruled them. Yet Solo now moved against the Jedi and the ideals of the Galactic Alliance Katarn served; the Galactic Alliance Guard [[Battle of Ossus (Second Galactic Civil War)|held the students hostage]] at the Jedi Academy on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. During the [[Battle of Kuat (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Kuat]], Skywalker confronted Jacen over his actions before pulling the Jedi Order out of the Galactic Alliance. In retaliation, the Galactic Alliance Guard soldiers on Ossus killed several Jedi Knights, mutilating [[Tionne Solusar]] and leaving her husband [[Kam Solusar|Kam]] for dead.<ref name="inferno" />

====Confrontation with a Sith Lord====
{{Quote|Watch out for lightsabers.''"<br />"''Watch out for durasteel rails.|Jagged Fel and Kyle Katarn, on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]|<ref name="fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>}}
[[File:LukeBenMaraFuneral-JvS.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Katarn, behind Luke Skywalker, attends Mara Jade Skywalker's funeral.]]
Katarn joined the other Jedi Masters on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], where they discussed their next actions against Jacen. Although Master Durron was in charge of the formulated mission to capture or kill, Katarn was selected by Skywalker as the team leader, and given a secondary objective to which only he was privy; a tracking device was to be placed upon Solo during their engagement with the powerful fallen Jedi and secret Dark Lord of the Sith. The team which accompanied Katarn included three Jedi Knights; Corran Horn's son [[Valin Horn|Valin]], the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Thann Mithric]] and the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Kolir Hu'lya]], as well as the young [[Seha Dorvald]], a former spy of Colonel Solo's. Katarn and his team infiltrated Coruscant's undercity and eventually assaulted Solo outside the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]].<ref name="fury" />

Solo was a formidable opponent, even for Katarn's skills, and the Jedi Master had to protect the weaker members of the team as he dueled the former Jedi Knight and now young Sith Lord. As Caedus claimed, Katarn was unaware of what he was fighting against, and the Sith's brutality during the duel saw the Jedi Master hard-pressed to win the advantage, though the mission itself was only a diversion to place the tracking device upon Solo. Forced to defend Hu'lya from Solo's attacks, Katarn sent his saber spinning at Jacen, only to have it deflected and return to his hand. When his opponent gestured towards him, Katarn prepared himself for a contest of telekinesis, though he was caught off-guard by Solo's tactic; the Sith Lord grasped a passing GAG speeder with the Force and sent it cannonading into the Jedi Master's back. Katarn was catapulted forward and impaled on Solo's blade. The Sith Lord pulled his lightsaber free and turned to finish off the rest of the team, unaware that the tracking device had been attached to his cloak by Dorvald, and that Katarn was still alive. Although a grief-consumed Mithric was slain by Solo, Horn and Dorvald dragged Katarn to safety while Hu'lya made good her escape. Unfortunately for the severely wounded Master, Solo's blade had punctured his lung, and Katarn's lowered immune system was unable to prevent the incursion of an infectious plague that the Commenori, opposed to the Galactic Alliance, had unleashed on Coruscant. However, against all odds, Kyle Katarn survived both the serious wound and the diseases he picked up in the undercity sewers. On his return to Endor he was healed by Cilghal, with yet another successful mission to his name. Katarn later regained enough of his strength to accompany the Jedi on their mission to the Corellian system, where they rescued the abducted [[Hapan]] [[Chume'da]] [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]] from Caedus' clutches.<ref name="fury" />

During the final battle with Darth Caedus and his forces in the Mist, Master Katarn was one of the many Jedi who took part in the assault. He, along with Sebatyne, Horn, Kyp, and Grand Master Skywalker, helped subdue the remaining Moffs after the death of Caedus.<ref name="Invincible">''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]''</ref>

====Crisis with the Galactic Alliance====
{{Quote|Get this thing off me.''"<br />"''I'm driving here, and that's ''Get this thing off me, please, Master Katarn.''|Jaina Solo and Katarn in pursuit of Valin Horn|<ref name="Outcast">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Kyle_Katarn_NJO.jpg|200px|thumb|left|Kyle Katarn as a Jedi Council member in the New Jedi Order]]
In [[43 ABY|43.5 ABY]], Katarn aided Jaina Solo in her attempt to capture the rogue Valin Horn, who had been struck with a mysterious [[Force psychosis|psychosis]]. Horn, who was also being followed by Master Skywalker, his son Ben, and Han and Leia Solo, news-crews, and bounty hunters, was eventually incapacitated by the Grand Master.<ref name="Outcast" />

When Master Skywalker was exiled, Katarn was present for the Grand Master and his son's farewell. Later, when Valin Horn was taken into GA custody, Katarn voiced his concern to Jaina Solo and two other Jedi Knights.<ref name="Outcast" />

During a Council meeting, Katarn appeared concerned for [[Jysella Horn]], daughter of his friend Corran Horn, after she went mad. When it was revealed what Jaina Solo and the [[Darkmeld]] team had done, Katarn was glad that [[Seff Hellin]] was now in the hands of the Jedi Order, but voiced concern over the fact that neither he nor the High Council had been informed by Cilghal, [[Tekli]], or Jaina Solo.<ref name="Omen">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]''</ref>

In later events, Kyle Katarn used the term "Grand Master" when referring to Kenth Hamner. However, it was in terms of sarcasm to the recent events concerning what to do with the rash of Jedi Knights who were going insane, as noted by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. He insisted that the Jedi support [[Tahiri Veila]] during her [[Trial of Tahiri Veila|trial]], which was agreed upon by masters Sebatyne and Cilghal.<ref name="Abyss">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]''</ref>

Katarn later went on to express his opinion about [[Natasi Daala]] threatening the Jedi with a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Siege of the New Jedi Temple|siege]]. When the siege happened, led by [[Belok Rhal]], Katarn pointed out that the Mandalorians were blocking every exit, secret and public, in and out of the Temple. He expressed concern for the populace of Coruscant, fearing they might get hurt if they tried to protest the siege. Hamner agreed, but didn't know how to work around this without stalling for time. Apprentice [[Kani Asari]] volunteered to go out and negotiate with Rhal. When Rhal murdered Kani in public, Katarn, [[Saba Sebatyne]], Corran, and Durron refused to let Hamner retrieve the body, that it wasn't safe for him to be out there with a deranged Mandalorian in charge of the siege.<ref name="Fate of the Jedi: Allies">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Allies]]''</ref>

Soon after these events, Master Katarn was among the Masters present when Han Solo proved to Chief of State Daala that the Jedi who were being driven mad had recovered, causing her to order the siege of the Jedi Temple lifted. However, she refused to release Valin and Jysella Horn back into the hands of the Jedi.<ref name="Vortex">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Vortex]]''</ref>

Later, during a meeting of the Council, tempers were flaring with both Daala's refusal to release the Horn Jedi and Kenth Hamner's lack of leadership during the situation. [[Octa Ramis]] insisted that they take military action and free the Horn's, by force if need be. Hamner refused. Katarn then spoke, saying that unless they wished to take this kind of abuse from Daala, they would have no choice but to take military action. During the course of the meeting both Han Solo and Leia Organa Solo interrupted the heated meeting to show the masters the revolution on Blaudu Sextus. When it showed that Mandalorians were in charge of putting down the revolt, Saba Sebatyne grew enraged and insisted that the Jedi do something. When none of the Masters know anything of the situation on Sextus, Katarn asked her several questions about the relationship of the Octusi, who were responsible for the revolt, and their owners. When things did not appear so black and white Katarn grew more interested in who was behind the revolt, rather than what the Jedi were going to do about it, and went so far as to suspect the newest member of the Council [[Barratk'l]] had something to do with it. The suspicion was soon wiped away when she not only insisted she had nothing to do with it, but Hamner backed her up. The meeting proceeded, with the Jedi asking Saba who among the Jedi should go and handle the situation of Sextus. When Katarn suggested [[Tesar Sebatyne]] and another Barabel Jedi, Saba grew angry at him that he would suggest those two, and refused to tell where they were currently, insisting that they had not gotten into anything that would cast the Jedi into bad light. The meeting ended shortly after when the masters stormed out; it was revealed that Kenth was not going to let the StealthX squadron launch.<ref name="Vortex" />

When the Masters were gathered to welcome back the Jedi who had been transported to the Transitory Mists, the events on Sextus were sparking slave revolts all over the galaxy and most Masters, including Katarn, felt that this was the will of the Force. When Kenth refused to allow anymore Jedi to be spared to help the fledgling revolts, Han Solo insisted that Kenth tell them about the deal with [[Nek Bwua'tu]]. When Katarn pressed about the deal, Kenth revealed that Bwua'tu had promised no military action would be taken against the Jedi as long as they didn't launch their Stealth X's from the Temple. This enraged the Council and caused Saba to call for a vote of No Confidence in the temporary Grand Master. Katarn was among those who called for his resignation.<ref name="Vortex" />

During the preparation for the launch, he was to lead a squadron of StealthXs, along with Kyp Durron, [[Octa Ramis]], and Barratk'l to assist Luke and Ben Skywalker against the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]]'s [[Sith armada (Lost Tribe of the Sith)|war fleet]]. When Kenth Hamner intercepted them and refused to let them leave, Saba Sebatyne told them to go to their launch stations. Kyle tried to implore his old friend Hamner to be reasonable and told him that he should not have tried to shoulder the weight of the entire Order on his back, which was why the Jedi had a Council. Hamner refused to listen, which resulted in a brutal [[Skirmish in the Operations Hangar|duel]] in which Sebatyne killed Hamner and reluctantly accepted his position as Grand Master until Skywalker's return. Katarn and the others were able to leave, then took part in the [[Battle in the Fallanassi village|rescue]] of the Skywalkers above [[Almania]]'s [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Pydyr]].<ref name="Vortex" />

Later, Master Katarn led a StealthX squadron against the Sith Armada in an ambush set up by Grand Master Luke Skywalker during [[Operation Shieldfall]]. The end of that battle led to the Sith Forces retreating with [[Abeloth]] and [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|''Ship'']]. Having no clue where Abeloth and her forces retreated too, Master Katarn was one of the groups charged with scouring the galaxy for any location that they may be hiding in. When these results turned up nothing, Katarn returned to Coruscant to both welcome back the returning Grand Master Skywalker and to take part in further strategies against the Lost Tribe.<ref name="Conviction">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Conviction]]''</ref>

When the Jedi left Coruscant for good, Master Katarn was one of the Jedi present on [[Upekzar]] to confront Abeloth. It turned out to be a trap however, and many Jedi were killed when the dark energy from that place exploded, also causing a Volcano to erupt. Most of the Jedi escaped.<ref name="Ascension" />

It is unknown what role, if any, Master Katarn played during the Jedi's assault on the Lost Tribe on Coruscant. He was among those present in Senator Wuul's office during the Council meeting to discuss further military action against the Lost Tribe and their new master, Abeloth. He expressed concern about Abeloth's origins, and was the only Master present to take Wynn Dorvan seriously when he said he killed Abeloth. When Tekli and Lowbacca informed the Council about the origins of Abeloth, Katarn gave several theories about what her endgame would be, and how Ben Skywalker figured into those plans. The Council meeting ended shortly after that when it was discovered that Ben had been captured by Abeloth and was fleeing Coruscant.<ref name="Apocalypse">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]]''</ref>

After Abeloth's death, Katarn was one of the masters present to welcome Luke back after the Grand Master awoke from his coma. When Luke told the masters his plan to move the Jedi off Coruscant completely, Katarn was one of the first to agree to Luke's way of thinking.<ref name="Apocalypse" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I knew then, when we said good-bye, that he was not ready for a full commitment to the Alliance. The emotional manipulation he endured at the hands of the Empire has hardened him against causes and made the idealistic, trusting young man into a deadly efficient saboteur whose loyalty must be earned rather than bought. One thing is certain. He may not totally trust the Alliance, but he will never forgive or forget what the Empire has done to him. [...] it is this hatred of the Empire that will insure his reliability to us.|Jan Ors|<ref name="DFmanual" />}}
[[File:KyleImp.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Cadet Leader Kyle Katarn, reflecting on the [[Battle of AX-456|carnage]] at [[AX-456]].]]
On the one hand, Katarn appeared very self-confident and possessed an almost cynical dry sense of humor. On the other hand, he often doubted himself and always gave every choice a good deal of thought. He was an independent and free-thinking person, but at the same time his primary concern was always the safety and well-being of those he cared for. An ever-present factor appeared to be fear—fear of dying, fear of doing the wrong thing. Considering himself unlucky, he didn't enjoy playing games.<ref name="sfte" />

In his later life, Kyle would admit that the prospect of being a Stormtrooper, feeling powerful and inspiring a faceless fear to the citizens, was something that he enjoyed. Katarn's blend of personalities made him a promising cadet leader while at the Imperial academy. [[Sergeant major/Legends|Sergeant Major]] [[Hong]] especially appreciated the young cadet's skills, admiring Katarn's rational approach to his assignments and his humility when asking the superior officer for advice.<ref name="sfte" />

During his [[Omega Exercise]], Katarn showed that his heart was softer than most other Imperials. He could not bring himself to order the execution of the Rebels his men had captured, instead choosing to let them go. Katarn later used his cynical sense of humor, reason, and concern for others to inspire his team when the mission went south. This enabled them to pull together and complete the mission.<ref name="sfte" />

[[File:Rebelagent.jpg|thumb|250px|left|Katarn with 8t88's arm clutching his father's holodisc.]]
When Katarn learned the truth about his father Morgan's death, his personality underwent a dramatic change. He became bitter and developed a stronger hatred for the Empire. Although he disliked Mon Mothma's psychological manipulation, he joined her cause not for her ideals but because of his father's memory. He accepted missions from the Alliance that were directed at the Empire to get the opportunity to personally kill Imperial agents and thwart their plans. He was no longer afraid of dying, but his fear of doing the wrong thing remained. His sensibilities persisted when he started working for the Alliance; he questioned himself about the morality of shooting two Stormtroopers from behind, but this soon stopped when one started turning towards him, and felt compelled to shoot them before a second thought.<ref name="sfte" />

Already soon after his graduation he realised negativity from the crowd on board the ''Star of Empire'', the hard looks and whispers because of his Imperial uniform, that caused him feeling alone, unwelcome and depressed. Even after his defection, Kyle had the same feelings on the ''New Hope'', thinking himself always as an outsider, this time from the other Rebels. He felt a connection with the ship he chose there, the ''Moldy Crow'', as he also felt like a bird away from its flock.<ref name="sfte" />

While throwing himself into one death-defying situation after another, Katarn was always careful not to harm innocents and cared greatly for the safety of his partner Jan Ors.

During his years as a Rebel agent, Katarn was a lone wolf, who preferred to work on his own, ignoring advice and dismissing help from Jan Ors, even while they worked together. Teamwork was, according to Ors, not one of his strengths; Katarn recklessly put himself in dangerous situations and Ors many times ran to his rescue, something which Katarn, usually found among fires, explosions and ruined buildings, considered unnecessary. Ors saw this attitude as a result of Katarn constantly wanting to impress others, despite his well known achievements. During the times when he requested Jan's help, or their brief dialogues during his extractions, he covered his anxiety and dangerous situations with light-hearted banters, like "How 'bout a ride?"<ref name="agent" />

Although Jan and Kyle owed their lives to each other (several times) Katarn was initially oblivious to her romantic feelings; It was not until the quest for the Valley of the Jedi when finally the pair declared their love, which had to pass through turbulent phases, not only through danger and death, but also coping with Kyle's dark mood swings; at some point his close bond with Mara Jade disrupted their relationship.<ref name="Saga2" />

[[File:KyleKatarnGalaxyOfHeroes.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Jedi Knight Kyle Katarn.]]
Sometimes Katarn showed a preference for [[Novanian grog/Legends|Novanian grog]].<ref name="RAaudio" />

Although a powerful warrior, and despite his reckless attitude, it seems that once Katarn discovered his Jedi powers, he started to keep a low profile; like his father before him,<ref name="sfte" /> he was reluctant to embrace [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] itself. The core of this reluctance was his fear of the dark side and distrust of his own motivation to use powers.<ref name="Saga1" /> He repeatedly denied the ways of the Force, at one time going so far as to completely sever his own connection to it. He knew that deep inside, he was driven by a lust for revenge and hatred against those that killed his father. He knew all too well that hatred was an emotion fed by the dark side.

He did call upon the dark side of the Force twice in the scramble for the Valley of the Jedi, as he had killed Maw in anger and used a dark side technique to defeat Boc; it was not only Rahn's guidance that kept him from succumbing, but also his love for Jan, and memory of his father.<ref name="Saga1" />

It was after the Dromund Kaas incident that he severed the bonds with the Force completely. His motivation for reconnecting with the Force was ultimately also motivated by revenge, as he sought to avenge Ors, whom he thought had been killed. But even in his darkest hours there would always be some light inside him. More often than not, he would spare the lives of even his worst enemies. When he learned that Ors was not dead, Katarn changed. His hatred was replaced with hope, and his lust for revenge was replaced with compassion.

[[File:Kyle_and_Jan.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Katarn developed a relationship with the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Jan Ors]] during the search for [[Valley of the Jedi|the Valley of the Jedi]].]]
Eventually, he came to accept his destiny as a Jedi Knight, arguing that no Force power is inherently dark or light, but that it fully depends on how and for what one uses it.<ref name="ja" /> Rather than being ashamed of his brushes with the dark side, he would openly share his experiences with his apprentices so that they would not make the same mistakes as he did. When Rosh Penin finally managed to reject the temptations of the dark side and returned to the ways of the Jedi and Jaden Korr managed to steer clear of the dark path altogether and emerged ''the'' hero of the crisis of the Disciples of Ragnos, Katarn finally realized that he was a worthy and powerful Jedi and a great teacher. His confidence increased—and it was clear that whatever hesitancy he may have once had was long since gone. During his time as a member of the Jedi High Council he kept a low profile still, most of the time listening and gathering facts, instead of getting caught up in the passions of his fellow Masters. However, it was noted that whenever he did speak, his comments were usually like a punch to the gut, in terms of how on point and honest they were.

Katarn never liked to wear traditional Jedi robes, instead choosing to wear the clothes he wore in his mercenary days. He still wore them even at formal gatherings, like Mara Jade's funeral. He didn't like to be referred to with titles like "Master Katarn", as titles made him uncomfortable, and preferred to be on a first name basis with his apprentices. In this way, he reflected the idea that he still felt a connection to the common people of the galaxy, and showing humility by preferring not to stand out.<ref name="ja" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Remember: abilities are not inherently good or evil, it's how you use them.|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="ja" />}}
From an early age Kyle showed big curiosity for the trade of his father, his use of tools, and displayed impressive technical and engineering talent, able to handle and troubleshoot anything in their homestead. Skills he acquired from his childhood in the farm was becoming one of the few Humans in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] fluent in the electronic language of [[Binary/Legends|droidspeak]],<ref name="agent" /> as well as a [[primitive binary code]], considered obsolete by then, whose combinations of two sounds represented letters of an alphabet.<ref name="JKaudio" />

[[File:Rebornpwned2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Katarn displays his superior skills in lightsaber combat, as he cuts down a Reborn in the [[Cairn Installation]].]]
In the Academy he further discovered his talents for cybernetics systems theory and information engineering.<ref name="DFmanual" /> Kyle also was trained in martial arts like fencing,<ref name="agent" /> shooting,<ref name="DFmanual" /> and piloting several kinds of support craft. He also was trained in breathing exercises that could lower his vital signs, flatten his brain activity and slow down his time perception.<ref name="sfte" /> After his defection, Kyle spent some time in a Rebel base populated by smugglers and pirates, and acquired skills of sabotage and subterfuge.<ref name="DFmanual" />

Katarn was adept at using a variety of weapons. His training gave him great skill in using Imperial weapons. This training, along with a natural intuition, allowed him to effectively use every weapon he came across in his travels. As competent with weaponry, Katarn had great accuracy when using blasters, rarely missing his shots and using all types of blasters from different shapes and sizes, shooting several adversaries with pistols, rifles, and even an [[Assault cannon/Legends|assault cannon]].<ref name="df" /> Once he acquired his first lightsaber, he was immediately able to wield it thanks to the fencing lessons he took in the Academy.<ref name="agent" />

Kyle’s martial arts abilities were very impressive. He was capable of fast, powerful jabs that were ultimately capable of felling Kell Dragons as shown when he fought and killed Kell Dragons with his bare hands aboard the Star Jewel. <ref>[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]</ref>He was also capable of powerful combos such as a headlock followed by a chokehold, choke-slam, and suplex; and a 3 punch combo ending in an uppercut.<ref>[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]</ref>

As with many other Force-sensitives, Katarn first discovered his talent in the form of some intuition, that often helped him to solve engineering problems once he calmed his mind. This clairvoyance became more puzzling during his Omega Exercise, as it manifested itself in the form of precognitive visions that momentarily saved his team.<ref name="SEaudio" /> After the Dark Trooper campaign he discovered himself something that he understood as the "gun trick", using [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] to fetch a weapon near him in difficult situations, something which he used on Nar Shaddaa. It was that time when the spirit of Qu Rahn, knowing that Kyle would stop Jerec from winning the Valley of the Jedi, came to him as a voice and taught him how to use the Force to save his life. The first time this happened, Kyle found himself wounded among various thugs, when he discovered how to [[Force speed/Legends|slow the time around him]].<ref name="agent" />

[[File:Kyle_Katarn_TCGGA.png|thumb|right|250px|Katarn was skilled with both a blaster and lightsaber.]]
Despite being almost completely self-trained, and with minimal lightsaber combat training, Katarn did learn many techniques from the spirits of Rahn and the ancient Jedi Tal in the Valley of the Jedi, such as the [[Flowing Water]] cut and the [[Falling Leaf]] attack. Thus, Katarn defeated seven trained Dark Jedi; [[Yun]], [[Gorc]], [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], [[Sariss]], [[Boc Aseca|Boc]], and then [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] himself—an amazing accomplishment for the neophyte he was at the time. Katarn was a master of the fifth form of lightsaber combat [[Form V/Legends|Djem So]]. Likewise, he held other forms such as [[Form I/Legends|Shii-Cho]] and [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]].<ref name="LotFPreview5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/LOTFpreview5|text=''Legacy of the Force'' Preview 5: Darth Krayt and Kyle Katarn, Jedi Battlemaster}}</ref><ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> On the odd occasion, he would develop a reverse Shien grip. He proved to be [[Ambidexterity|ambidextrous]], wielding a weapon in his right hand as well as his left.<ref name="eao" /> When he became Battlemaster, his study of lightsaber combat led him to mix elements of several forms into his own personal style.<ref name="JATM" />

[[File:Kyle_sariss.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Katarn showed great skill dueling Sariss, the best lightsaber duelist of Jerec's Dark Jedi.]]
Katarn was strong in Force powers, such as [[Tutaminis|Force absorb]] and [[Force barrier/Legends|Force protection]] (which he demonstrated during his mission with Jaden Korr); but despite his fear of the dark side, Katarn utilized the dark powers of [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force grip]] and [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] even as a Jedi Master.<ref name="ja" />

During the Reborn crisis caused by Desann, Katarn had to revisit the Valley of the Jedi to quickly restore his connection to the Force, which increased his raw Force potential as a side-effect. It should be noted however that his motivation was once more revenge; the seeming death of Jan Ors. Katarn showed mastery of the Force by relearning powers of both the light and dark side of the Force in a relatively short amount of time. Katarn showed a strong will, resisting the many taunts and mind tricks Desann used on him in the Yavin temple.<ref name="jk2" />

Katarn became one of the [[New Jedi Order]]'s most respected Jedi Masters. During the events of the Disciples of Ragnos Crisis, Katarn had already become one of the strongest swordsmen in the New Jedi Order and successfully trained Jaden Korr, arguably the most famous hero during those events.<ref name="jk2" /> An extraordinary swordsman, Katarn became the New Jedi Order's foremost Battlemaster. His general mastery of the Force was incredible and surpassed all but the strongest Jedi Masters. Even Darth Caedus acknowledged Katarn's abilities, something he rarely did.<ref name="fury" />

Katarn demonstrated a comfortable understanding of [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]] with his old friend [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], when he and his apprentice Jaden Korr met up with the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] at the [[Mission to Mos Eisley (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Mos Eisley]].<ref name="jk2" />

=== Force powers ===
Kyle was known to use [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] such as [[Force jump/Legends|Force Jump]], [[Force speed/Legends|Force Speed]], [[Force sight|Force Seeing]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Pull|Force Pull]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Push|Force Push]], [[Force healing/Legends|Force Healing]], [[Force cloak|Force Persuasion]], [[Force blinding|Force Blinding]], [[Tutaminis|Force Absorb]], [[Force barrier/Legends|Force Protection]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Throw|Force Throw]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force Grip]], [[Force lightning/Legends|Force Lightning]], [[Force Destruction]], [[Deadly Sight|Force Deadly Sight]], [[Dopplegänger|Force Projection]], [[Telekinesis/Legends#Saber Throw|Saber Throw]], [[Farsight/Legends|Far sight]], [[Force barrier/Legends|Force Defense]], and [[Mind trick/Legends|Jedi Mind Trick]]. He could also possibly use [[Force drain/Legends|Force Drain]], and [[Force Enrage|Force Rage]].<ref name=":0">[[Dark Forces/Jedi Knight|Dark Forces/Jedi Knight series]]</ref> He was also notably skilled in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|using the Force for lightsaber offense and defense]]. Katarn had also once used an arcane dark side ability known as the [[Darkshear|Spear of Midnight Black]].<ref name="JKaudio" />

==Equipment==
===Weapons===
Kyle Katarn's preferred weapon was a [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]] that had been given to him by his father. A weapon modified from a [[Bryar rifle]]. He continued to carry and use it, as a backup weapon, unlike other Jedi who normally eschewed blasters, even after becoming a Jedi and building his first lightsaber. Katarn briefly used an [[Imperial repeater rifle|Imperial Repeater rifle]], [[Jeron fusion cutter|Jeron Fusion Cutter]], [[packered mortar gun]], [[Assault cannon/Legends|Assault Cannon]], [[rail detonator]] gun, [[Seeker Rail Detonator|seeker rail detonator]] gun, [[Bowcaster/Legends|Wookiee Bowcaster]], [[Stouker concussion rifle|Stouker]] [[Concussion rifle/Legends|concussion rifle]], [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44]], [[carbonite gun]], [[Imperial Heavy Repeater]], [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifle]], [[E-11 electroscope|E-11 Electroscope]], [[DXR-6 disruptor rifle]], [[FC-1 flechette launcher/Legends|FC-1 flechette launcher]], [[PLX-2M portable missile launcher]], and [[Destructive Electromagnetic Pulse 2 ion carbine]]. He also used [[sequencer charge]]s, [[Remote detonator|Manual Sequencers]], [[I.M. mine|I.M. Mines]], [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], [[Flash grenade/Legends|flash bombs]], [[Detonation pack|detonation packs]], and [[Laser trip mine/Legends|laser trip mines]]. Kyle was also known to fight bare handed or with a [[Stun baton/Legends|stun baton]]. During his career, Katarn used a [[Personal energy shield/Legends|personal energy shield]] with a belt-mounted shield generator called the [[Military Mobility Shield]].<ref name=":0" /> In his mercenary days, Katarn frequently wore body armor underneath his trademark coat. He was also one of the few non-Mandalorians to own a set of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian Armor]], which he recovered when he was captured by Jabba.<ref name="df" />This is also likely the same armor he wore during the [[Battle of Altyr V (Galactic Civil War)|defense of Altyr V]] and his [[Mission to Dromund Kaas (New Republic)|mission to Dromund Kaas]].<ref>[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]</ref>

====Lightsabers====
{{Main|Qu Rahn's lightsaber}}
{{Main|Yun's lightsaber}}
{{Main|Kyle Katarn's third lightsaber}}
[[File:Kyle_Katarn_saber.jpg|thumb|276x276px|Kyle Katarn's third lightsaber]]
Throughout his life, Kyle Katarn wielded at least three lightsabers. His [[Qu Rahn's lightsaber|first lightsaber]] originally belonged to Jedi Master Qu Rahn and produced a green blade. Rahn passed it on to Morgan Katarn, who in turn gave it to his son, Kyle Katarn, who used it in his [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] with the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Yun]], [[Gorc]], [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], and [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]]. This weapon was destroyed by the Dark Jedi, [[Boc Aseca]], while Katarn journeyed into the Valley of the Jedi; however, one of the Dark Jedi, Yun, turned from the Dark Side and blocked a death blow to Katarn from Sariss. Yun was killed, and Katarn took [[Yun's lightsaber]] for the rest of his journey through the Valley of the Jedi to duel and kill [[Sariss]], Boc, and [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]].<ref name="JK" /> This weapon had a yellow blade and may have been the same blade he later used while training Mara Jade, though at that time it appeared to have a more orange hue.<ref name="MOTS" />

Following the events on Dromund Kaas, Katarn attended the Jedi Praxeum, where he constructed a [[Kyle Katarn's third lightsaber|new blue-bladed lightsaber]], which had two handgrips and a longer hilt than most average lightsabers. This lightsaber was Kyle's first official lightsaber, as his previous two belonged to other individuals.<ref name="jk2" />

=== <big>Gear</big> ===
Kyle would use a variety of items in his missions such as his trusty [[Head lamp/Legends|field light]], [[Glowrod/Legends|glowrod]], [[Infrared goggles/Legends|infrared goggles]], [[Breath mask/Legends|gas mask]], [[Boot/Legends|ice cleats]], [[Bacta tank/Legends|portable bacta tanks]], [[Blast vest/Legends|armored]] [[Flak vest/Legends|vests]], [[Reflective armor|shield supercharges]], [[Power cell/Legends|weapon]] [[Blaster gas canister/Legends|supercharges]], [[Medpac/Legends|revives]], [[Gear bag|smuggler backpacks]], [[Pauldron/Legends|stormtrooper backpacks]], [[Medkit|medkits]], [[Blaster power pack/Legends|ammunition]], [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|personal shield generators]], [[Battery/Legends|battery packs]], [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]], [[Mark VII "Inquisitor" Series Seeker Droid|Arakyd Mark VII Inquisitors]], [[Autoturret/Legends|assault sentries]], and [[Power converter/Legends|power converters]].<ref name=":0" />  

===Ships===
The ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'', an [[HWK-290 light freighter/Legends|HWK-290 light freighter]], was the first ship acquired by Kyle Katarn after joining the Rebel Alliance. He first used it during his [[Mission to Danuta]]. He took an immediate liking to the ship due to its name, as it reflected his feelings on his own status in the galaxy. In later missions, the ''Crow'' was piloted by Katarn's partner Jan Ors. It was eventually destroyed during his mission to the Valley of the Jedi.<ref name="JK" />

After the destruction of the ''Moldy Crow'', Kyle Katarn and his partner Jan Ors continued their career with the ''[[Raven's Claw]]; a [[Courier/Legends|light courier]] modified for combat''. Ors piloted the ship for Katarn, ensuring that he could be inserted and extracted safely for his missions. Kyle also briefly used a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Lambda Class Shuttle]] <ref>[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]</ref>and a [[Shuttle/Legends|Jedi Praxeum Shuttle]].<ref>[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
===Portrayal===
[[File:Kyle-Jan-Preprod-photo.jpg|200px|thumb|[[Jason Court]] and [[Angela Harry]] in a preproduction still for ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''.]]
Kyle Katarn was voiced by [[Nick Jameson]] in ''Dark Forces'' and his appearance appears on hand-drawn stills in the cutscenes.

Actor [[Jason Court]] portrayed the character in the full-motion video sequences of ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''. For the close-ups of Katarn's hands and feet, actor and VFX artist [[C. Andrew Nelson]] doubled for Court. The in-game model was based on Court's appearance. Likewise, in [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith|''Mysteries of Sith'']], [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast|''Jedi Outcast'']], and ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'', Katarn is exclusively a polygonal model, without [[Wikipedia:Full motion video|full motion video]] scenes, made to look like a slightly older Court. In these appearances, he is voiced by [[Rino Romano]] (in ''Mysteries of the Sith'') and [[Jeff Bennett]] (in ''Jedi Outcast'' and ''Jedi Academy''). In ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' he is voiced by [[Brian Bascle]] and for the audio dramatizations of the ''Dark Forces'' novellas, he is portrayed by [[Randal Berger]]. Jeff Bennett's voice acting in ''Jedi Outcast'' and ''Jedi Academy'' makes him the only person to have voiced Katarn more than once.

===Continuity===
====Appearance====
Most recent portrayals of Katarn are based on Jason Court's appearance no matter the timeline; whereas the original ''Dark Forces'', set around 1 ABY during Katarn's early career in the Rebellion, displays him as young, beardless, with a brown jacket, later works set in that period show him with Court's older bearded appearance. This includes the ''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'' novella illustrated by [[Dean Williams]], set around the same time with ''Dark Forces'', but Kyle is anachronistically bearded and sports the civilian attire he is seen in ''Dark Forces II: Jedi Knight''. This is also the case with the [[Star Wars: Empire at War|''Empire at War'']] and [[Star Wars: Lethal Alliance|''Lethal Alliance'']] video games.

====Alternative endings====
{{Quote|Why do I need you, Jerec, when I can take all the power of the Valley myself?|Kyle Katarn {{C|non-canon}}|<ref name="JK" />}}
[[File:EmperorKatarn.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Emperor Katarn takes his throne.]]
Katarn's story has alternative outcomes in some of the games in which he appears. Although the above-mentioned story is canonical, the player has the option to follow the alternative dark side path in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''.

In this version, Katarn killed Jan Ors when prompted and became the rival of Jerec. Yun, not particularly touched as Katarn proved to be just another fallen Jedi, fought him alone after Katarn escaped the falling cargo ship. In this version Katarn is able to land the ''Moldy Crow'' safely. He corrected his previous mistake and killed Yun in a second lightsaber duel. After he defeated Jerec in the Valley of the Jedi, Katarn absorbed its power and took his position on Coruscant as Emperor of the galaxy, with Sariss at his side. He ordered her to destroy a small Rebel [[Danuta Uprising|uprising on Danuta]], which was ironically the location of his first major mission for the Rebel Alliance. Then, he took out the [[Hologram/Legends|holo]] of his father saying, "I want you to remember, while you're at the academy, how very proud I am of you," the same holo he had been reflecting on when his mission against Jerec first began. He then dropped it on the floor and crushed it with his boot, removing all his connections to the past and the light.

The same option is available to Jaden Korr in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''. In this version, Korr turned to the dark side by killing Penin instead of staying in the light by forgiving him. Korr later dueled Katarn, overpowering his master with the Scepter of Ragnos he had stolen from Tavion, and escaped. Reflecting on the incident with Luke Skywalker, Katarn questioned whether or not he himself should be a Jedi, taking a leave of absence to apprehend Korr.

The alternative ending of ''Jedi Academy'', although not canonical, also gives some insight into Katarn's psyche in a case of failure and disappointment. Failure empowers his existing disappointment; he seems guilty for having lost two apprentices (Penin is dead, and Korr becomes a Dark Jedi), and confesses to Skywalker that he is unsure if he should be a Jedi Master at all. However, as a final desperate act, he moved to personally find Korr and correct his failure.

===Popularity===
[[File:Kylefigure.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Kyle Katarn 2009 Action Figure.]]
Although created only for the needs of a video game, Katarn became surprisingly popular, having the most appearances of all of the Inaugural Class Jedi Masters. One reason is that the computer games he appears in became quite successful and widely referenced. This success has been further developed by comparisons to martial artist and action star [[Wikipedia:Chuck Norris|Chuck Norris]], which have prompted Star Wars-themed variations of the popular [[Wikipedia:Chuck Norris facts|Chuck Norris facts]] meme.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=threads/24965494|board=Literature|thread=Kyle Katarn is so tough…|archivedate=20170723173358}}</ref><ref>{{JCFCite|url=threads/26105309|board=Literature|thread=The Kyle Katarn Facts thread.|archivedate=20170723210321}}</ref><ref>[[Star Wars Insider 100|''Star Wars Insider'' 100]], "100 Best Things About Star Wars": "There is no chin under Kyle Katarn's beard. There is only another fist." (Also online at {{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20071221.html|text=The 2007 Databank 100}})</ref>

Even after the establishment of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity as a way to bring forth a new ''[[Star Wars]]'' canon,<ref name="End of EU">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> Katarn has remained a notable character.<ref name="Reddit">{{DMCite|dm=File:MaraAndKyleConfirmation-RedditDM.jpg|creator=Dylan Innes|date=2025-9-20}}</ref> A bearded individual in [[Part Three (Pieces of the Past)|episode three]] of the [[2025]] non-canon series ''[[LEGO Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy - Pieces of the Past]]''<ref name="PotP">{{PiecesOfThePast|Part Three}}</ref> was intended by the development team to be Katarn,<ref name="Reddit" /> and was later identified as Kyle Katarn in an article on [[StarWars.com]].<ref name="Easter Eggs">{{SW|url=news/lego-rebuild-the-galaxy-pieces-of-the-past-deep-cuts|text=Our Favorite Deep Cuts and Fun Facts from ''LEGO Star Wars: Rebuild the Galaxy - Pieces of the Past'' - Updated}}</ref>

===Other===
Originally, the protagonist of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' was to be [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and the game's story was to follow the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. However, the developers of ''Dark Forces'' realized that this decision would add constraints to the storyline and gameplay, so the character of Kyle Katarn was created instead.<ref name="DB-Katarn, Kyle">{{DB|character|kylekatarn|Katarn, Kyle}}</ref>

[[File:KyleBTS-DB.jpg|thumb|200px|left|Kyle Katarn concept drawing.]]
According to the ''Dark Forces'' review in PC Gamer, Katarn was intended to be a "blank" game character, more or less like other customizable game protagonists (cf. Jaden Korr or [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]); he would have a customizable name, a mysterious unmentioned past and an unseen face, in order to help players identify themselves with the protagonist. It seems that this concept was abandoned since his face, name and background history are told in the manual and the narrative of the final product. However, the games still demand to enter a player name in order to save their progress.

Kyle Katarn appears as one of the hero character units in ''Empire at War'' and is one of few Expanded Universe characters in the game. His special abilities are "[[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]" and "sprint." He has no Force powers since the game takes place before his discovery of his connection to the Force.

Katarn was originally slated to die in the novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'', but the scene was removed at the last minute at the request of [[LucasArts]], ostensibly for reasons related to ''Jedi Academy'' despite that game's earlier placement on the timeline.<ref name="Celebration III">{{JCFCite|url=posts/19359853|board=Literature|thread=Celebration III EU News|user=Errant_Venture|date=2005-04-23|time=10:34 PM|archivedate=20210117124700|quote=Katarn was supposed to die in SbS as well, but that was canned due to LA wanting to keep him alive for reasons unknown. They said it was due to Jedi Academy, but that takes place before SbS so that didn't make any sense.}}</ref><ref name="Chee Twitter">{{Twitter|HolocronKeeper|status/1074892930622451712|[[Leland Chee]]|archivedate=20210309103439|quote=What an awesome game but it will always remind me of how perilously close we came to killing off Kyle Katarn in the New Jedi Order novel, Star By Star.}}</ref> The removal of this scene allowed Katarn to finally make his comic book debut in "[[Equals & Opposites]]"—almost three years after the release of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' in the United States.<ref name="eao" />
